Please note that any changes targeting the original code should remove the line number, colon, and leading space.\n1: \"\"\"\n2: core/task_queue.py — Single-Threaded Task Queue for OOM Prevention\n3: ===================================================================\n4: A lightweight, background job queue that ensures Open Claw skills run\n5: sequentially. This replaces the old concurrent `subprocess.Popen` logic\n6: in `inbox_daemon.py` which could easily crash the system (OOM) when\n7: multiple files arrive simultaneously.\n8: \"\"\"\n9: \n10: from datetime import datetime\n11: import json\n12: import os\n13: import queue\n14: import shutil\n15: import subprocess\n16: import threading\n17: import time\n18: from typing import Dict, List, Optional\n19: \n20: from core.orchestration.event_bus import DomainEvent, EventBus\n21: from core.utils.log_manager import build_logger\n22: \n23: # Use workspace root to locate the data directory for quarantine\n24: from core.utils.workspace import get_workspace_root\n25: \n26: _workspace_root = get_workspace_root()\n27: _logger = build_logger(\n28:     \"OpenClaw.TaskQueue\", log_file=os.path.join(_workspace_root, \"logs\", \"task_queue.log\")\n29: )\n30: \n31: \n32: class LocalTaskQueue:\n33:     _instance = None\n34:     _lock = threading.Lock()\n35: \n36:     def __new__(cls):\n37:         with cls._lock:\n38:             if cls._instance is None:\n39:                 cls._instance = super().__new__(cls)\n40:                 cls._instance._init()\n41:             return cls._instance\n42: \n43:     def _init(self):\n44:         self.q = queue.Queue()\n45:         self.max_retries = 3\n46:         self.timeout_sec = 3600  # 1 hour max per pipeline task to prevent hangin\n<truncated 5923 bytes>\n] 達到最大重試次數，放棄任務: %s\", name)\n173:             self._quarantine_file(task, error_msg)\n174:         else:\n175:             backoff_sec = 5 * (2 ** task[\"retry_count\"])\n176:             _logger.warning(\n177:                 \"🔄 [TaskQueue] 準備重試任務 (等待 %ds 後排入佇列尾端): %s\", backoff_sec, name\n178:             )\n179: \n180:             try:\n181:                 from core.services.telegram_bot import send_message\n182: \n183:                 send_message(\n184:                     error_msg\n185:                     + f\"\\n將於 {backoff_sec} 秒後進行第 {task['retry_count'] + 1} 次重試...\"\n186:                 )\n187:             except ImportError:\n188:                 pass\n189: \n190:             time.sleep(backoff_sec)\n191:             self.q.put(task)\n192: \n193:     def enqueue(\n194:         self,\n195:         name: str,\n196:         cmd: List[str],\n197:         cwd: str,\n198:         filepath: Optional[str] = None,\n199:         skill: Optional[str] = None,\n200:         chain: Optional[List[str]] = None,\n201:         subject: str = \"Default\",\n202:         env: Optional[Dict[str, str]] = None,\n203:     ):\n204:         \"\"\"\n205:         Add a task to the queue.\n206:         \"\"\"\n207:         self.q.put(\n208:             {\n209:                 \"name\": name,\n210:                 \"cmd\": cmd,\n211:                 \"cwd\": cwd,\n212:                 \"filepath\": filepath,\n213:                 \"skill\": skill,\n214:                 \"chain\": chain or [],\n215:                 \"subject\": subject,\n216:                 \"env\": env,\n217:                 \"retry_count\": 0,\n218:             }\n219:         )\n220: \n221:         _logger.info(\"📥 [TaskQueue] 任務已排入佇列 (等候中: %d): %s\", self.q.qsize(), name)\n222: \n223:     def join(self):\n224:         \"\"\"Block until all tasks are completed.\"\"\"\n225:         self.q.join()\n226: \n227: \n228: # Migration of `open(..., \\\"w\\\")` to `AtomicWriter`\\n\\nWe conducted a thorough codebase search for any remaining `open(..., \\\"w\\\")` calls across `core/` and `skills/`. The search returned occurrences only in the following contexts:\\n\\n- **Lockfiles**: Used alongside `fcntl.flock` (e.g. `core/orchestration/run_all_pipelines.py`, `core/state/state_backend.py`, `core/state/state_manager.py`). These MUST use raw `open()` because atomic writes operate on temporary files which break `fcntl` file descriptor locks.\\n- **Null Devices**: `with open(os.devnull, \\\"w\\\")` in `audio_transcriber` for silencing third-party module outputs.\\n- **Test / Dev Scripts**: `ops/`, `scripts/`, `tests/`.\\n\\nConclusion: The `AtomicWriter` migration is **fully complete** for production data logic. No further migrations are required.\\n\\n## 2. Universal Handoff / End-to-End Routing Verification\\n\\nWe verified that the `student_researcher` output successfully routes to `knowledge_compiler`. \\n\\n**Findings**:\\n- When `Phase2Synthesis` writes the final `_research.md` file, it correctly calls `self.emit_completed(...)`.\\n- `RouterAgent` catches the `PipelineCompleted` domain event on the local EventBus.\\n- `RouterAgent._on_pipeline_completed` retrieves the `chain` and `subject` metadata, then physically moves the file from `student_researcher/input/` to `knowledge_compiler/input/`.\\n- We patched the local `scripts/simulate_routing.py` mock `LocalTaskQueue` to strictly verify this without incurring heavy PDF/LLM overhead. The test passed, correctly enqueuing the `knowledge_compiler` pipeline downstream after `student_researcher` finished.\\n\\n> [!TIP]\\n> **Orchestration Note**: Handoffs relying on `EventBus` require `RouterAgent` and the pipelines to operate within the same Python process/Daemon (e.g., via `inbox_daemon.py` or the `LocalTaskQueue` daemon worker). {"step_index":606,"source":"MODEL","type":"PLANNER_RESPONSE","status":"DONE","created_at":"2026-05-29T04:55:27Z","content":"I have completed the remaining tasks on the V9.24 Incubator Code Review checklist and validated the end-to-end routing.\n\nHere is a summary of the actions taken:\n\n### 1. Verification of `open(..., \"w\")` to `AtomicWriter` Migration\nI performed a comprehensive check of all remaining `open(..., \"w\")` calls in the `core/` and `skills/` directories. I found that all instances of `open(..., \"w\")` are intentional and **should not** be migrated to `AtomicWriter`:\n* **Lockfiles** (`core/orchestration/run_all_pipelines.py`, `core/state_backend.py`): These files are used in conjunction with `fcntl.flock()`. `AtomicWriter` writes to a temporary file and renames it, which inherently breaks low-level file descriptor locks.\n* **Null Streams**: Output streams being piped into `/dev/null` (e.g., inside `audio_transcriber` silencing external libraries).\n* **Test/Dev Code**: Instances strictly within `ops/`, `scripts/`, or `tests/`.\n\nConclusion: The `AtomicWriter` migration is **fully implemented** for all production data storage paths.\n\n### 2. End-to-End Routing Verification (`student_researcher` ➡️ `knowledge_compiler`)\nI tested the \"Universal Handoff\" orchestration between these two skills.\n\n* **Findings**: In the previous steps, we correctly modified `p02_synthesis.py` to trigger `self.emit_completed()`.\n* **Validation**: I fixed and executed the `simulate_routing.py` orchestrator script, which confirms that `RouterAgent._on_pipeline_completed()` successfully listens to the `student_researcher` completion event, extracts the subject (`Incubator`), dynamically retrieves the subsequent node from the execution chain (`knowledge_compiler`), and **physically moves the `.md` files** from `data/student_researcher/input/Incubator` to `data/knowledge_compiler/input/Incubator`.\n* **Note on Execution Context**: Keep in mind that event-driven handoffs depend on the global `EventBus`, meaning that the origin script and `RouterAgent` must run inside the same python daemon (e.g., via the system `task_queue` or `inbox_daemon`). If you run the script manually (standalone from the CLI via `uv run ...`), the memory boundaries isolate the events, so it won't automatically handoff!\n\nI have updated [task.md](file:///Users/limchinkun/.gemini/antigravity/brain/32729395-ac4d-4d84-8be5-537c18482c2d/task.md) and created a final [walkthrough.md](file:///Users/limchinkun/.gemini/antigravity/brain/32729395-ac4d-4d84-8be5-537c18482c2d/walkthrough.md) documenting these checks. The V9.24 Incubator fixes are now fully complete! Please let me know if there are any next phases or modifications you'd like to work on.","thinking":"**Prioritizing Tool Specificity**\n\nI'm focusing intently on tool selection to streamline my approach. I'm actively avoiding 'cat' for file manipulation, recognizing its inefficiency. Also, grep_search is the go-to alternative to embedded grep, except when it is absolutely required. Listing is being approached with a new and better perspective - I'm no longer reaching for \"ls\".\n\n\n"}

Please note that any changes targeting the original code should remove the line number, colon, and leading space.\n1: \"\"\"\n2: skills/knowledge_compiler/scripts/phases/p02_extract_graph.py  (P3-2)\n3: ======================================================================\n4: Phase 2: Wikilink → Entity-Relation Graph Extraction.\n5: \n6: Parses compiled wiki/*.md files from p01_compile, extracts:\n7:   - Entities from [[wikilink]] syntax\n8:   - Relations from the \"## 延伸連結\" section\n9:   - Tags from #Hashtag entries\n10: \n11: Then upserts everything into the configured GraphStore backend\n12: (NetworkX local or Neo4j prod).\n13: \n14: CLI:\n15:     python p02_extract_graph.py [--subject SUBJECT] [--force]\n16: \n17: After this phase runs, core.hybrid_retriever can augment vector queries\n18: with 1-hop graph neighbours (P3-3).\n19: \"\"\"\n20: \n21: from __future__ import annotations\n22: \n23: import os\n24: import re\n25: \n26: from core import PipelineBase\n27: from core.ai.graph_store import get_graph_store\n28: \n29: # Regex patterns\n30: _WIKILINK_RE = re.compile(r\"\\[\\[([^\\[\\]|#]+?)(?:\\|[^\\]]+?)?\\]\\]\")\n31: _HASHTAG_RE = re.compile(r\"(?<!\\w)#([A-Za-z\\u4e00-\\u9fff][A-Za-z0-9\\u4e00-\\u9fff_-]*)\")\n32: _RELATION_SECTION_RE = re.compile(r\"##\\s*延伸連結(.*?)(?=\\n##|\\Z)\", re.DOTALL)\n33: _RELATION_LINE_RE = re.compile(\n34:     r\"([\\u4e00-\\u9fff A-Za-z0-9_-]+?)\\s+((?:RELATED_TO|IS_A|PART_OF|DEPENDS_ON|ENABLES|CITES|延伸|關聯|屬於|包含|依賴))\\s+([\\u4e00-\\u9fff A-Za-z0-9_-]+)\",\n35:     re.IGNORECASE,\n36: )\n37: \n38: # Default relation when only a wikilink is found with no explicit relation\n39: DEFAULT_RELATION = \"RELATED_TO\"\n40: \n41: \n42: clas\n<truncated 6178 bytes>\npile)\")\n160:             return\n161: \n162:         files = [\n163:             os.path.join(self.wiki_dir, f)\n164:             for f in os.listdir(self.wiki_dir)\n165:             if f.endswith(\".md\") and f != \"INDEX.md\"\n166:         ]\n167: \n168:         if not files:\n169:             self.warning(\"⚠️ wiki/ 目錄為空，無可處理的檔案。\")\n170:             return\n171: \n172:         self.info(f\"📄 共找到 {len(files)} 個 wiki 筆記，開始抽取實體與關係...\")\n173:         for filepath in sorted(files):\n174:             self._process_file(filepath)\n175: \n176:         # Persist graph state\n177:         self.graph.close()\n178: \n179:         if hasattr(self.graph, \"node_count\"):\n180:             self.info(\n181:                 f\"\\n✨ 圖譜抽取完成！節點: {self.graph.node_count}, 邊: {self.graph.edge_count}\"\n182:             )\n183:         else:\n184:             self.info(\"\\n✨ 圖譜抽取完成！\")\n185: \n186:         # Publish event for downstream consumers (P1-1 EventBus)\n187:         try:\n188:             from core.orchestration.event_bus import DomainEvent, EventBus\n189: \n190:             EventBus.publish(\n191:                 DomainEvent(\n192:                     name=\"KnowledgeCompiled\",\n193:                     source_skill=\"knowledge_compiler\",\n194:                     payload={\"wiki_dir\": self.wiki_dir, \"file_count\": len(files)},\n195:                 )\n196:             )\n197:         except Exception:\n198:             pass  # EventBus is optional — never block the pipeline\n199: \n200: \n201: if __name__ == \"__main__\":\n202:     import argparse\n203: \n204:     parser = argparse.ArgumentParser(description=\"Knowledge Compiler — Phase 2: Graph Extraction\")\n205:     parser.add_argument(\"--subject\", help=\"Filter by subject\")\n206:     parser.add_argument(\"--force\", action=\"store_true\")\n207:     args = parser.parse_args()\n208:     Phase2ExtractGraph().run(force=args.force, subject=args.subject)\n209: \nThe above content shows the entire, complete file contents of the requested file.\n"}

{"step_index":656,"source":"MODEL","type":"VIEW_FILE","status":"DONE","created_at":"2026-05-29T05:32:40Z","content":"Created At: 2026-05-29T05:32:40Z\nCompleted At: 2026-05-29T05:32:40Z\nFile Path: `file:///Users/limchinkun/Desktop/local-workspace/openclaw-sandbox/core/ai/graph_store.py`\nTotal Lines: 309\nTotal Bytes: 11910\nShowing lines 1 to 309\nThe following code has been modified to include a line number before every line, in the format: <line_number>: <original_line>. Please note that any changes targeting the original code should remove the line number, colon, and leading space.\n1: \"\"\"\n2: core/graph_store.py — Knowledge Graph Abstraction Layer (P3-1)\n3: =============================================================\n4: Provides a unified GraphStore interface over two backends:\n5:   - NetworkX  (local / dev / test — zero external dependencies beyond networkx)\n6:   - Neo4j     (production — requires neo4j Python driver + a running Neo4j instance)\n7: \n8: The active backend is selected at runtime from config.yaml:\n9:     graph:\n10:       backend: \"networkx\"   # or \"neo4j\"\n11:       neo4j:\n12:         uri: \"bolt://localhost:7687\"\n13:         user: \"neo4j\"\n14:         password: \"password\"\n15:       persist_path: \"state/graph.gpickle\"   # for NetworkX backend\n16: \n17: Usage:\n18:     from core.ai.graph_store import get_graph_store\n19:     gs = get_graph_store(workspace_root, skill_name=\"knowledge_compiler\")\n20: \n21:     gs.upsert_entity(\"認知心理學\", labels=[\"Concept\", \"Psychology\"], props={\"source\": \"notes.md\"})\n22:     gs.upsert_relation(\"認知心理學\", \"RELATED_TO\", \"工作記憶\")\n23:     neighbours = gs.get_neighbours(\"認知心理學\", max_hops=1)\n24:     # [{\"name\": \"工作記憶\", \"relation\": \"RELATED_TO\"}, ...]\n25: \n26: Requirements (install only what you need):\n27:     pip install networkx                      # always safe\n28:     pip install neo4j                         # only for production backend\n29: \"\"\"\n30: \n31: from __future__ import annotations\n32: \n33: import logging\n34: import os\n35: import threading\n36: from typing import Any, Dict, List, Optional, Protocol, runtime_checkable\n37: \n38: _logger = loggi\n<truncated 9805 bytes>\nMATCH (n {name: $name}) RETURN count(n) AS c\", name=name)\n264:             return result.single()[\"c\"] > 0\n265: \n266:     def close(self) -> None:\n267:         self._driver.close()\n268:         _logger.info(\"[GraphStore/Neo4j] Connection closed.\")\n269: \n270: \n271: # ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n272: # Factory\n273: # ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n274: \n275: \n276: def get_graph_store(workspace_root: str, skill_name: str = \"knowledge_compiler\") -> Any:\n277:     \"\"\"Instantiate and return the configured GraphStore backend.\n278: \n279:     Reads config from skills/<skill_name>/config/config.yaml under the\n280:     ``graph:`` section. Falls back to NetworkX if no config is present.\n281: \n282:     Args:\n283:         workspace_root: Absolute path to the sandbox root.\n284:         skill_name:     Skill whose config.yaml is consulted.\n285: \n286:     Returns:\n287:         A GraphStore-compatible instance (NetworkXGraphStore or Neo4jGraphStore).\n288:     \"\"\"\n289:     from core.config.config_manager import ConfigManager  # type: ignore[import]\n290: \n291:     cfg_mgr = ConfigManager(workspace_root, skill_name)\n292:     graph_cfg: Dict[str, Any] = cfg_mgr.get_section(\"graph\") or {}\n293:     backend = graph_cfg.get(\"backend\", \"networkx\").lower()\n294: \n295:     if backend == \"neo4j\":\n296:         neo_cfg = graph_cfg.get(\"neo4j\", {})\n297:         return Neo4jGraphStore(\n298:             uri=neo_cfg.get(\"uri\", \"bolt://localhost:7687\"),\n299:             user=neo_cfg.get(\"user\", \"neo4j\"),\n300:             password=os.environ.get(\"NEO4J_PASSWORD\", neo_cfg.get(\"password\", \"\")),\n301:         )\n302: \n303:     # Default: NetworkX\n304:     persist_path = graph_cfg.get(\n305:         \"persist_path\",\n306:         os.path.join(workspace_root, \"skills\", skill_name, \"data\", \"state\", \"graph.json\"),\n307:     )\n308:     return NetworkXGraphStore(persist_path=persist_path)\n309: \nThe above content shows the entire, complete file contents of the requested file.\n"}

{"step_index":659,"source":"MODEL","type":"VIEW_FILE","status":"DONE","created_at":"2026-05-29T05:32:46Z","content":"Created At: 2026-05-29T05:32:46Z\nCompleted At: 2026-05-29T05:32:47Z\nFile Path: `file:///Users/limchinkun/Desktop/local-workspace/openclaw-sandbox/core/ai/hybrid_retriever.py`\nTotal Lines: 218\nTotal Bytes: 8689\nShowing lines 1 to 218\nThe following code has been modified to include a line number before every line, in the format: <line_number>: <original_line>. Please note that any changes targeting the original code should remove the line number, colon, and leading space.\n1: \"\"\"\n2: core/hybrid_retriever.py — Hybrid RAG Retriever (P3-3)\n3: ======================================================\n4: Vector top-K ∪ GraphStore 1-hop expansion → LLM rerank → ordered context.\n5: \n6: Config (config.yaml):\n7:     hybrid_retriever:\n8:       vector_top_k: 10\n9:       graph_max_hops: 1\n10:       rerank: true\n11:       rerank_model: \"qwen3:8b\"\n12:       rerank_top_n: 5\n13:     vector_db:\n14:       path: \"skills/telegram_kb_agent/state/chroma_db\"\n15: \n16: Requirements:\n17:     pip install networkx chromadb\n18: \"\"\"\n19: \n20: from __future__ import annotations\n21: \n22: import logging\n23: import os\n24: import re\n25: from typing import Any, Dict, List, Optional\n26: \n27: _logger = logging.getLogger(\"OpenClaw.HybridRetriever\")\n28: \n29: \n30: class HybridRetriever:\n31:     \"\"\"Vector top-K ∪ Graph 1-hop → optional LLM rerank.\"\"\"\n32: \n33:     def __init__(self, workspace_root: str, skill_name: str = \"telegram_kb_agent\"):\n34:         self.workspace_root = workspace_root\n35:         self.skill_name = skill_name\n36:         self._chroma_client: Any = None\n37:         self._collection: Any = None\n38:         self._graph_store: Any = None\n39:         self._llm: Any = None\n40: \n41:         from core.config.config_manager import ConfigManager  # type: ignore[import]\n42: \n43:         cfg = ConfigManager(workspace_root, skill_name)\n44:         hr = cfg.get_section(\"hybrid_retriever\") or {}\n45:         self._vector_top_k: int = hr.get(\"vector_top_k\", 10)\n46:         self._graph_max_hops: int = hr.get(\"graph_max_hops\", 1)\n47:         self.\n<truncated 6133 bytes>\n     seen_texts.add(key)\n178:                 deduped.append(p)\n179: \n180:         # Step 3: LLM rerank\n181:         if self._rerank and not skip_rerank and len(deduped) > top_n:\n182:             try:\n183:                 deduped = self._llm_rerank(question, deduped, top_n)\n184:             except Exception as exc:\n185:                 _logger.warning(\"[HybridRetriever] Rerank failed: %s\", exc)\n186:                 deduped = deduped[:top_n]\n187:         else:\n188:             deduped = deduped[:top_n]\n189: \n190:         return deduped\n191: \n192:     def _llm_rerank(\n193:         self, question: str, passages: List[Dict[str, Any]], top_n: int\n194:     ) -> List[Dict[str, Any]]:\n195:         numbered = \"\\n\\n\".join(f\"[{i + 1}] {p['text'][:300]}\" for i, p in enumerate(passages))\n196:         prompt = (\n197:             f\"問題：{question}\\n\\n候選段落：\\n{numbered}\\n\\n\"\n198:             f\"選出最相關的 {top_n} 段，輸出逗號分隔序號，例如：3,1,5。只輸出序號。\"\n199:         )\n200:         raw = self._get_llm().generate(model=self._rerank_model, prompt=prompt).strip()\n201:         indices = [int(x) - 1 for x in re.findall(r\"\\d+\", raw) if 0 < int(x) <= len(passages)]\n202:         reranked = [passages[i] for i in indices]\n203:         mentioned = set(indices)\n204:         for i, p in enumerate(passages):\n205:             if len(reranked) >= top_n:\n206:                 break\n207:             if i not in mentioned:\n208:                 reranked.append(p)\n209:         return reranked[:top_n]\n210: \n211:     def format_context(self, passages: List[Dict[str, Any]]) -> str:\n212:         \"\"\"Format passages as an LLM context block.\"\"\"\n213:         parts = []\n214:         for i, p in enumerate(passages, 1):\n215:             tag = \"📊 圖譜\" if p[\"origin\"] == \"graph\" else \"📄 向量\"\n216:             parts.append(f\"[{i}] {tag} (來源: {p['source']})\\n{p['text']}\")\n217:         return \"\\n\\n---\\n\\n\".join(parts)\n218: \nThe above content shows the entire, complete file contents of the requested file.\n"}

{"step_index":662,"source":"MODEL","type":"VIEW_FILE","status":"DONE","created_at":"2026-05-29T05:32:55Z","content":"Created At: 2026-05-29T05:32:55Z\nCompleted At: 2026-05-29T05:32:55Z\nFile Path: `file:///Users/limchinkun/Desktop/local-workspace/openclaw-sandbox/skills/telegram_kb_agent/scripts/indexer.py`\nTotal Lines: 145\nTotal Bytes: 4261\nShowing lines 1 to 145\nThe following code has been modified to include a line number before every line, in the format: <line_number>: <original_line>. Please note that any changes targeting the original code should remove the line number, colon, and leading space.\n1: import glob\n2: import os\n3: import sys\n4: \n5: import chromadb\n6: import requests\n7: \n8: # Core Bootstrap\n9: from core.utils.path_builder import PathBuilder\n10: \n11: \n12: def get_config():\n13:     import yaml\n14: \n15:     config_path = os.path.join(os.path.dirname(__file__), \"..\", \"config\", \"config.yaml\")\n16:     with open(config_path, encoding=\"utf-8\") as f:\n17:         return yaml.safe_load(f)\n18: \n19: \n20: def chunk_text(text: str, chunk_size: int, overlap: int):\n21:     \"\"\"Simple sliding window chunker.\"\"\"\n22:     chunks = []\n23:     start = 0\n24:     while start < len(text):\n25:         end = start + chunk_size\n26:         chunks.append(text[start:end])\n27:         if end >= len(text):\n28:             break\n29:         start += chunk_size - overlap\n30:     return chunks\n31: \n32: \n33: def get_embedding(text: str, model: str, api_url: str):\n34:     \"\"\"Get embedding from Ollama.\"\"\"\n35:     url = f\"{api_url}/embeddings\"\n36:     response = requests.post(url, json={\"model\": model, \"prompt\": text})\n37:     if response.status_code == 200:\n38:         return response.json().get(\"embedding\")\n39:     else:\n40:         raise RuntimeError(f\"Embedding failed: {response.text}\")\n41: \n42: \n43: def main():\n44:     print(\"🚀 啟動 Knowledge Base Indexer...\")\n45: \n46:     cfg = get_config()\n47:     rag_cfg = cfg.get(\"rag\", {})\n48:     chunk_size = rag_cfg.get(\"chunk_size\", 1000)\n49:     overlap = rag_cfg.get(\"chunk_overlap\", 200)\n50:     model = rag_cfg.get(\"embed_model\", \"nomic-embed-text\")\n51:     api_url = cfg.get(\"runtime\", {}).get(\"olla\n<truncated 1356 bytes>\nel, api_url)\n89: \n90:                 doc_ids.append(doc_id)\n91:                 embeddings.append(emb)\n92:                 documents.append(chunk)\n93:                 metadatas.append({\"filename\": filename, \"chunk_index\": i})\n94: \n95:                 print(f\"  ✅ 已處理: {doc_id}\")\n96:             except Exception as e:\n97:                 print(f\"  ❌ 處理失敗: {doc_id} - {e}\")\n98: \n99:     if doc_ids:\n100:         print(f\"💾 正在將 {len(doc_ids)} 筆向量資料存入 ChromaDB...\")\n101:         # Upsert automatically overwrites if the ID exists\n102:         collection.upsert(\n103:             ids=doc_ids, embeddings=embeddings, documents=documents, metadatas=metadatas\n104:         )\n105:         print(\"✅ 知識庫索引建立完成！\")\n106:     else:\n107:         print(\"⚠️ 沒有任何資料被索引。\")\n108: \n109: \n110: if __name__ == \"__main__\":\n111:     try:\n112:         main()\n113:         print(\"🏁 Pipeline 執行完畢。\")\n114:         try:\n115:             import subprocess\n116: \n117:             subprocess.run(\n118:                 [\n119:                     \"osascript\",\n120:                     \"-e\",\n121:                     'display notification \"Pipeline 執行完畢\" with title \"Open-Claw\"',\n122:                 ],\n123:                 check=False,\n124:             )\n125:         except Exception:\n126:             pass\n127:     except KeyboardInterrupt:\n128:         print(\"\\n🛑 使用者手動中斷執行 (KeyboardInterrupt)\")\n129:         try:\n130:             import subprocess\n131: \n132:             subprocess.run(\n133:                 [\n134:                     \"osascript\",\n135:                     \"-e\",\n136:                     'display notification \"Execution Interrupted\" with title \"Open-Claw\"',\n137:                 ],\n138:                 check=False,\n139:             )\n140:         except Exception:\n141:             pass\n142:         import sys\n143: \n144:         sys.exit(130)\n145: \nThe above content shows the entire, complete file contents of the requested file.\n"}

{"step_index":664,"source":"MODEL","type":"PLANNER_RESPONSE","status":"DONE","created_at":"2026-05-29T05:32:55Z","thinking":"**Prioritizing Tool Specificity**\n\nI'm focusing intently on tool selection to streamline my approach. I'll need to analyze `hybrid_retriever.py` to identify points for integration, focusing on the current graph expansion logic and its relation to vector search results to enable deep integration as per the request.\n\n\n**Integrating Graph and ChromaDB**\n\nI'm now contemplating a deep integration between the graph and ChromaDB. I'm also ensuring to prioritize the user's need, whether they desire only a Phase B plan, or desire to run batch synthesis right away.\n\n\n","tool_calls":[{"name":"write_to_file","args":{"ArtifactMetadata":"{\"ArtifactType\":\"implementation_plan\",\"RequestFeedback\":true,\"Summary\":\"Implementation plan for Phase B: ChromaDB and NetworkX Deep Integration for Graph RAG.\"}","CodeContent":"\"# Phase B: Memory & Graph RAG (ChromaDB + NetworkX Deep Integration)\\n\\n本計畫旨在完成 `TASKS.md` 中剩餘的中優先級（Medium Priority）項目，核心為推進 **Phase B 的知識圖譜與向量資料庫深度整合**，並執行後續的測試。\\n\\n## Proposed Changes\\n\\n### 1. 執行批次合成與索引重建\\n在開始重構前，先確保系統資料是最新的。\\n- **Note Generator**: 執行 `uv run skills/note_generator/scripts/run_all.py --subject 助人技巧 --force` 生成完整的筆記內容。\\n- **ChromaDB Indexer**: 執行 `uv run skills/telegram_kb_agent/scripts/indexer.py` 重新建立向量索引。\\n\\n### 2. 重構 GraphStore 實體向量化 (Deep Integration)\\n目前 `hybrid_retriever.py` 中的 Graph 檢索使用的是「正則表達式硬匹配 (Regex Exact Match)」來從問題中提取關鍵字，並去 NetworkX 中尋找實體：\\n'''python\\ncandidates = re.findall(r\\\"[\\\\u4e00-\\\\u9fff]{2,10}|[A-Z][a-zA-Z]{2,}\\\", question)\\n'''\\n這種作法非常脆弱（若用戶輸入同義詞則找不到節點）。\\n\\n#### [MODIFY] [hybrid_retriever.py](file:///Users/limchinkun/Desktop/local-workspace/openclaw-sandbox/core/ai/hybrid_retriever.py)\\n我們將進行真正的深度整合（Deep Integration）：\\n1. **語意實體檢索 (Semantic Entity Retrieval)**：當用戶提問時，不只用 Regex 切字，而是將 Regex 提取的候選詞，甚至直接將 Question 本身進行 Embedding。\\n2. 由於目前的 ChromaDB (`wiki_knowledge` collection) 存儲的是文本分塊 (Chunks)，我們可以：\\n   - 方案 A: 透過 `knowledge_compiler` 在提取 Graph 的時候，同時將 Entity 列表建立另一個 ChromaDB Collection (`wiki_entities`)，這樣 Query 時可以直接 Semantic Search 找到最接近的 Graph Node 作為 Graph Expansion 的起點。\\n   - 方案 B: 在檢索時，使用 LLM 快速提取 Query 中的精確實體，再從 Graph 查詢（較耗時但精準）。\\n   - 方案 C: 先透過 ChromaDB 檢索出 Top-K 的 Text Chunks，並從這些 Text Chunks \n<truncated 1670 bytes>","Description":"\"Write Phase B implementation plan\"","IsArtifact":"true","Overwrite":"true","TargetFile":"\"/Users/limchinkun/.gemini/antigravity/brain/32729395-ac4d-4d84-8be5-537c18482c2d/implementation_plan.md\"","toolAction":"\"Writing Phase B implementation plan\"","toolSummary":"\"Write implementation plan\""}}]}

{"step_index":704,"source":"MODEL","type":"VIEW_FILE","status":"DONE","created_at":"2026-05-29T05:36:36Z","content":"Created At: 2026-05-29T05:36:36Z\nCompleted At: 2026-05-29T05:36:36Z\nFile Path: `file:///Users/limchinkun/Desktop/local-workspace/openclaw-sandbox/core/utils/path_builder.py`\nTotal Lines: 201\nTotal Bytes: 7128\nShowing lines 1 to 60\nThe following code has been modified to include a line number before every line, in the format: <line_number>: <original_line>. Please note that any changes targeting the original code should remove the line number, colon, and leading space.\n1: \"\"\"\n2: core/path_builder.py — Config-Driven Canonical Path Construction\n3: ================================================================\n4: Resolves all directory paths for a skill from its config.yaml `paths:` section.\n5: \n6: Schema (all values relative to data/<skill_name>/):\n7:     paths:\n8:       input:  \"input/...\"        # canonical input root\n9:       output: \"output\"           # canonical output root\n10:       state:  \"state\"            # pipeline state JSON + checklist.md\n11:       logs:   \"logs\"             # log files\n12: \n13:       phases:                    # becomes self.dirs[key] in Phase scripts\n14:         <key>: \"relative/path\"\n15:         ...\n16: \n17: Fallback:\n18:     If config.yaml is missing or has no `paths:` section, PathBuilder\n19:     falls back to the built-in defaults defined in _DEFAULTS so existing\n20:     skills without a `paths:` block continue to work.\n21: \n22: Zero `if skill_name ==` branches — any new skill only needs a\n23: `paths:` section in its config.yaml.\n24: \"\"\"\n25: \n26: from __future__ import annotations\n27: \n28: from functools import cached_property\n29: import os\n30: from typing import Dict\n31: \n32: # ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n33: # Built-in defaults (used when config.yaml is unavailable).\n34: # Structured identically to the YAML schema for consistency.\n35: # ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n36: _DEFAULTS: Dict[str, Dict] = {\n37:     \"audio_transcriber\": {\n38:         \"input\": \"input\",\n39:         \"output\": \"output\",\n40:         \"state\": \"state\",\n41:         \"resume\": \"state/resume\",\n42:         \"logs\": \"logs\",\n43:         \"phases\": {\n44:             \"p0\": \"input\",\n45:             \"p1\": \"output/01_transcript\",\n46:             \"p2\": \"output/02_proofread\",\n47:             \"p3\": \"output/03_merged\",\n48:             \"p4\": \"output/04_highlighted\",\n49:             \"p5\": \"output/05_notion_synthesis\",\n50:         },\n51:     },\n52:     \"doc_parser\": {\n53:         \"input\": \"input\",\n54:         \"output\": \"output\",\n55:         \"state\": \"state\",\n56:         \"resume\": \"state/resume\",\n57:         \"logs\": \"logs\",\n58:         \"phases\": {\n59:             \"inbox\": \"input\",\n60:             \"processed\": \"output/01_processed\",\nThe above content does NOT show the entire file contents. Step 2: Graph expansion\n147:         if not skip_graph:\n148:             try:\n149:                 graph = self._get_graph()\n150:                 candidates = re.findall(r\"[\\u4e00-\\u9fff]{2,10}|[A-Z][a-zA-Z]{2,}\", question)\n151:                 seen: set = set()\n152:                 for entity in candidates:\n153:                     if not graph.entity_exists(entity):\n154:                         continue\n155:                     for nbr in graph.get_neighbours(entity, self._graph_max_hops):\n156:                         name = nbr[\"name\"]\n157:                         if name not in seen:\n158:                             seen.add(name)\n159:                             passages.append(\n160:                                 {\n161:                                     \"text\": f\"[圖譜] {entity} {nbr['relation']} {name}\",\n162:                                     \"source\": \"knowledge_graph\",\n163:                                     \"score\": 0.6,\n164:                                     \"origin\": \"graph\",\n165:                                 }\n166:                             )\n167:                 _logger.info(\"[HybridRetriever] Graph: %d passages.\", len(seen))\n168:             except Exception as exc:\n169:                 _logger.warning(\"[HybridRetriever] Graph expansion failed: %s\", exc)\n170: \n171:         # Deduplicate\n172:         seen_texts: set = set()\n173:         deduped = []\n174:         for p in sorted(passages, key=lambda x: x[\"score\"], reverse=True):\n175:             key = p[\"text\"][:120]\n176:             if key not in seen_texts:\n177:                 seen_texts.add(key)\n178:                 deduped.append(p)\n179: \n180:         # Please note that any changes targeting the original code should remove the line number, colon, and leading space.\n1: # Open Claw — Knowledge Compiler Configuration\n2: \n3: models:\n4:   default: \"qwen3:8b\"     # primary — best quality for entity/link extraction\n5:   fallback: \"qwen3:8b\"     # fallback — circuit breaker / OOM\n6: \n7: phase01:\n8:   model: qwen3:8b\n9:   chunk_size: 4000  # primary\n10:   # model: \"qwen3:8b\"        # fallback\n11:   options:\n12:     temperature: 0\n13:     num_predict: 2048\n14: \n15: runtime:\n16:   ollama:\n17:     api_url: \"http://127.0.0.1:11434/api/generate\"\n18:     timeout_seconds: 600\n19:     retries: 3\n20:     backoff_seconds: 5\n21: \n22: # Output paths\n23: output:\n24:   wiki_dir: \"\"          # Leave blank to use default: data/<skill>/wiki/\n25: \n26: # P3-1: Knowledge Graph Backend\n27: # backend: \"networkx\"  — local/dev (zero config, persists to graph.gpickle)\n28: # backend: \"neo4j\"     — production (requires neo4j driver + running instance)\n29: graph:\n30:   backend: \"networkx\"\n31:   persist_path: \"state/graph.gpickle\"\n32:   neo4j:\n33:     uri: \"bolt://localhost:7687\"\n34:     user: \"neo4j\"\n35:     password: \"\"        # Set via OPENCLAW_NEO4J_PASSWORD env var in prod\n36: \n37: # P3-3: Hybrid Retrieval (used by core/hybrid_retriever.py)\n38: hybrid_retriever:\n39:   vector_top_k: 10\n40:   graph_max_hops: 1\n41:   rerank: true\n42:   rerank_model: \"qwen3:14b\"   # primary\n43:   # rerank_model: \"qwen3:8b\" # fallback\n44:   rerank_top_n: 5\n45: \nThe above content shows the entire, complete file contents of the requested file.\n"}

{"step_index":800,"source":"MODEL","type":"VIEW_FILE","status":"DONE","created_at":"2026-05-29T05:51:15Z","content":"Created At: 2026-05-29T05:51:15Z\nCompleted At: 2026-05-29T05:51:15Z\nFile Path: `file:///Users/limchinkun/Desktop/local-workspace/openclaw-sandbox/skills/telegram_kb_agent/scripts/indexer.py`\nTotal Lines: 145\nTotal Bytes: 4261\nShowing lines 1 to 60\nThe following code has been modified to include a line number before every line, in the format: <line_number>: <original_line>. Please note that any changes targeting the original code should remove the line number, colon, and leading space.\n1: import glob\n2: import os\n3: import sys\n4: \n5: import chromadb\n6: import requests\n7: \n8: # Core Bootstrap\n9: from core.utils.path_builder import PathBuilder\n10: \n11: \n12: def get_config():\n13:     import yaml\n14: \n15:     config_path = os.path.join(os.path.dirname(__file__), \"..\", \"config\", \"config.yaml\")\n16:     with open(config_path, encoding=\"utf-8\") as f:\n17:         return yaml.safe_load(f)\n18: \n19: \n20: def chunk_text(text: str, chunk_size: int, overlap: int):\n21:     \"\"\"Simple sliding window chunker.\"\"\"\n22:     chunks = []\n23:     start = 0\n24:     while start < len(text):\n25:         end = start + chunk_size\n26:         chunks.append(text[start:end])\n27:         if end >= len(text):\n28:             break\n29:         start += chunk_size - overlap\n30:     return chunks\n31: \n32: \n33: def get_embedding(text: str, model: str, api_url: str):\n34:     \"\"\"Get embedding from Ollama.\"\"\"\n35:     url = f\"{api_url}/embeddings\"\n36:     response = requests.post(url, json={\"model\": model, \"prompt\": text})\n37:     if response.status_code == 200:\n38:         return response.json().get(\"embedding\")\n39:     else:\n40:         raise RuntimeError(f\"Embedding failed: {response.text}\")\n41: \n42: \n43: def main():\n44:     print(\"🚀 啟動 Knowledge Base Indexer...\")\n45: \n46:     cfg = get_config()\n47:     rag_cfg = cfg.get(\"rag\", {})\n48:     chunk_size = rag_cfg.get(\"chunk_size\", 1000)\n49:     overlap = rag_cfg.get(\"chunk_overlap\", 200)\n50:     model = rag_cfg.get(\"embed_model\", \"nomic-embed-text\")\n51:     api_url = cfg.get(\"runtime\", {}).get(\"ollama\", {}).get(\"api_url\", \"http://127.0.0.1:11434/api\")\n52: \n53:     pb = PathBuilder(\n54:         os.path.abspath(os.path.join(os.path.dirname(__file__), \"..\", \"..\", \"..\")),\n55:         \"telegram_kb_agent\",\n56:     )\n57:     pb.ensure_directories()\n58: \n59:     wiki_dir = os.path.abspath(os.path.join(pb.base_dir, \"..\", \"wiki\"))\n60:     db_path = os.path.join(pb.canonical_dirs[\"state\"], \"chroma_db\")\nThe above content does NOT show the entire file contents. If you need to view any lines of the file which were not shown to complete your task, call this tool again to view those lines.\n"}

{"step_index":828,"source":"MODEL","type":"VIEW_FILE","status":"DONE","created_at":"2026-05-29T06:34:25Z","content":"Created At: 2026-05-29T06:34:25Z\nCompleted At: 2026-05-29T06:34:26Z\nFile Path: `file:///Users/limchinkun/Desktop/local-workspace/openclaw-sandbox/skills/telegram_kb_agent/scripts/indexer.py`\nTotal Lines: 145\nTotal Bytes: 4261\nShowing lines 61 to 145\nThe following code has been modified to include a line number before every line, in the format: <line_number>: <original_line>. Please note that any changes targeting the original code should remove the line number, colon, and leading space.\n61: \n62:     print(f\"📦 初始化 ChromaDB 於: {db_path}\")\n63:     client = chromadb.PersistentClient(path=db_path)\n64:     collection = client.get_or_create_collection(name=\"wiki_knowledge\")\n65: \n66:     md_files = glob.glob(os.path.join(wiki_dir, \"**\", \"*.md\"), recursive=True)\n67:     print(f\"📄 找到 {len(md_files)} 個 Wiki 檔案，準備處理...\")\n68: \n69:     doc_ids = []\n70:     embeddings = []\n71:     documents = []\n72:     metadatas = []\n73: \n74:     for file_path in md_files:\n75:         filename = os.path.basename(file_path)\n76:         if filename == \"INDEX.md\":\n77:             continue\n78: \n79:         with open(file_path, encoding=\"utf-8\") as f:\n80:             content = f.read()\n81: \n82:         chunks = chunk_text(content, chunk_size, overlap)\n83: \n84:         for i, chunk in enumerate(chunks):\n85:             doc_id = f\"{filename}_chunk_{i}\"\n86: \n87:             try:\n88:                 emb = get_embedding(chunk, model, api_url)\n89: \n90:                 doc_ids.append(doc_id)\n91:                 embeddings.append(emb)\n92:                 documents.append(chunk)\n93:                 metadatas.append({\"filename\": filename, \"chunk_index\": i})\n94: \n95:                 print(f\"  ✅ 已處理: {doc_id}\")\n96:             except Exception as e:\n97:                 print(f\"  ❌ 處理失敗: {doc_id} - {e}\")\n98: \n99:     if doc_ids:\n100:         print(f\"💾 正在將 {len(doc_ids)} 筆向量資料存入 ChromaDB...\")\n101:         # Upsert automatically overwrites if the ID exists\n102:         collection.upsert(\n103:             ids=doc_ids, embeddings=embeddings, documents=documents, metadatas=metadatas\n104:         )\n105:         print(\"✅ 知識庫索引建立完成！\")\n106:     else:\n107:         print(\"⚠️ 沒有任何資料被索引。\")\n108: \n109: \n110: if __name__ == \"__main__\":\n111:     try:\n112:         main()\n113:         print(\"🏁 Pipeline 執行完畢。\")\n114:         try:\n115:             import subprocess\n116: \n117:             subprocess.run(\n118:                 [\n119:                     \"osascript\",\n120:                     \"-e\",\n121:                     'display notification \"Pipeline 執行完畢\" with title \"Open-Claw\"',\n122:                 ],\n123:                 check=False,\n124:             )\n125:         except Exception:\n126:             pass\n127:     except KeyboardInterrupt:\n128:         print(\"\\n🛑 使用者手動中斷執行 (KeyboardInterrupt)\")\n129:         try:\n130:             import subprocess\n131: \n132:             subprocess.run(\n133:                 [\n134:                     \"osascript\",\n135:                     \"-e\",\n136:                     'display notification \"Execution Interrupted\" with title \"Open-Claw\"',\n137:                 ],\n138:                 check=False,\n139:             )\n140:         except Exception:\n141:             pass\n142:         import sys\n143: \n144:         sys.exit(130)\n145: \nThe above content does NOT show the entire file contents. If you need to view any lines of the file which were not shown to complete your task, call this tool again to view those lines.\n"}

Please note that any changes targeting the original code should remove the line number, colon, and leading space.\n70:     \"input\": \"input\",\n71:     \"output\": \"output\",\n72:     \"state\": \"state\",\n73:     \"resume\": \"state/resume\",\n74:     \"logs\": \"logs\",\n75: }\n76: \n77: \n78: class PathBuilder:\n79:     \"\"\"\n80:     Constructs absolute directory paths for an OpenClaw skill.\n81: \n82:     Construction is cheap — YAML is loaded lazily on first access via\n83:     `cached_property`. All derived paths are absolute strings.\n84:     \"\"\"\n85: \n86:     def __init__(self, workspace_root: str, skill_name: str):\n87:         self.workspace_root = os.path.abspath(workspace_root)\n88:         self.skill_name = skill_name\n89:         self.base_dir = os.path.join(self.workspace_root, \"data\", skill_name)\n90: \n91:     # ------------------------------------------------------------------\n92:     # Config loading (lazy, cached)\n93:     # ------------------------------------------------------------------\n94: \n95:     @cached_property\n96:     def _raw_paths_cfg(self) -> Dict:\n97:         \"\"\"\n98:         Load and return the `paths:` subtree from config.yaml, or fall\n99:         back to the built-in defaults for this skill.\n100:         \"\"\"\n101:         cfg_file = self.config_file  # skills/<skill>/config/config.yaml\n102:         if os.path.exists(cfg_file):\n103:             try:\n104:                 import yaml\n105: \n106:                 with open(cfg_file, encoding=\"utf-8\") as f:\n107:                     data = yaml.safe_load(f) or {}\n108:                 paths_block = data.get(\"paths\")\n109:                 if isinstance(paths_block, dict):\n110:                     return paths_block\n111:             except Exception:\n112:                 pass  # fall through to defaults\n113: \n114:         # Config missing or has no `paths:` — use built-in defaults\n115:         return _DEFAULTS.get(self.skill_name, _GENERIC_DEFAULT)\n116: \n117:     # ------------------------------------------------------------------\n118:     # Helpers\n119:     # ------------------------------------------------------------------\n120: \n121:     def _resolve(self, rel_path: str) -> str:\n122:         \"\"\"Join rel_path with base_dir, returning an absolute path.\"\"\"\n123:         return os.path.normpath(os.path.join(self.base_dir, rel_path))\n124: \n125:     # ------------------------------------------------------------------\n126:     # Static properties (no YAML needed)\n127:     # ------------------------------------------------------------------\n128: \n129:     @property\n130:     def config_dir(self) -> str:\n131:         return os.path.join(self.workspace_root, \"skills\", self.skill_name, \"config\")\n132: \n133:     @property\n134:     def config_file(self) -> str:\n135:         return os.path.join(self.config_dir, \"config.yaml\")\n136: \n137:     @property\n138:     def prompt_file(self) -> str:\n139:         return os.path.join(self.config_dir, \"prompt.md\")\n140: \n141:     # ------------------------------------------------------------------\n142:     # Config-driven path properties\n143:     # ------------------------------------------------------------------\n144: \n145:     @property\n146:     def canonical_dirs(self) -> Dict[str, str]:\n147:         \"\"\"\n148:         Returns the five canonical directories expected by PipelineBase:\n149:         input, output, state, resume, logs.\n150:         \"\"\"\nThe above content does NOT show the entire file contents. If you need to view any lines of the file which were not shown to complete your task, call this tool again to view those lines.\n"}

P1-6: Resolve wiki_dir from config.yaml (output.wiki_dir) instead of hardcoded ../wiki\n19:         output_cfg = self.config_manager.get_section(\"output\") or {}\n20:         default_wiki = os.path.abspath(os.path.join(self.base_dir, \"..\", \"wiki\"))\n21:         self.wiki_dir = os.path.realpath(output_cfg.get(\"wiki_dir\", default_wiki))\n22:         os.makedirs(self.wiki_dir, exist_ok=True)\n23: \n24:         self.model_name = self.config_manager.get_nested(\"models\", \"default\") or \"qwen3:8b\"\n25: \n26:         # P1-6: Load system prompt from prompt.md (externalised — no more inline string literals)\n27:         self._prompt_tpl = \"\"\n28:         if os.path.exists(self.prompt_file):\n29:             try:\n30:                 with open(self.prompt_file, encoding=\"utf-8\") as f:\n31:                     raw = f.read()\n32:                 # Cross-Semester Linking\n192:             related_titles = self._find_similar_notes(content, safe_title)\n193:             if related_titles:\n194:                 related_links = \"\\n\".join(\n195:                     [f\"- ⚡ Related to past note: [[{t}]]\" for t in related_titles]\n196:                 )\n197:                 final_doc += f\"\\n\\n## 延伸連結\\n{related_links}\\n\"\n198: \n199:             # WikiLink Guard: downgrade dead [[Links]] before writing to vault\n200:             final_doc, _ = self._validate_wikilinks(final_doc)\n201: \n202:             AtomicWriter.write_text(out_path, final_doc)\n203: \n204:             # Please note that any changes targeting the original code should remove the line number, colon, and leading space.\n1: \"\"\"\n2: skills/knowledge_compiler/scripts/phases/p02_extract_graph.py  (P3-2)\n3: ======================================================================\n4: Phase 2: Wikilink → Entity-Relation Graph Extraction.\n5: \n6: Parses compiled wiki/*.md files from p01_compile, extracts:\n7:   - Entities from [[wikilink]] syntax\n8:   - Relations from the \"## 延伸連結\" section\n9:   - Tags from #Hashtag entries\n10: \n11: Then upserts everything into the configured GraphStore backend\n12: (NetworkX local or Neo4j prod).\n13: \n14: CLI:\n15:     python p02_extract_graph.py [--subject SUBJECT] [--force]\n16: \n17: After this phase runs, core.hybrid_retriever can augment vector queries\n18: with 1-hop graph neighbours (P3-3).\n19: \"\"\"\n20: \n21: from __future__ import annotations\n22: \n23: import os\n24: import re\n25: \n26: from core import PipelineBase\n27: from core.ai.graph_store import get_graph_store\n28: \n29: # Regex patterns\n30: _WIKILINK_RE = re.compile(r\"\\[\\[([^\\[\\]|#]+?)(?:\\|[^\\]]+?)?\\]\\]\")\n31: _HASHTAG_RE = re.compile(r\"(?<!\\w)#([A-Za-z\\u4e00-\\u9fff][A-Za-z0-9\\u4e00-\\u9fff_-]*)\")\n32: _RELATION_SECTION_RE = re.compile(r\"##\\s*延伸連結(.*?)(?=\\n##|\\Z)\", re.DOTALL)\n33: _RELATION_LINE_RE = re.compile(\n34:     r\"([\\u4e00-\\u9fff A-Za-z0-9_-]+?)\\s+((?:RELATED_TO|IS_A|PART_OF|DEPENDS_ON|ENABLES|CITES|延伸|關聯|屬於|包含|依賴))\\s+([\\u4e00-\\u9fff A-Za-z0-9_-]+)\",\n35:     re.IGNORECASE,\n36: )\n37: \n38: # Default relation when only a wikilink is found with no explicit relation\n39: DEFAULT_RELATION = \"RELATED_TO\"\n40: \n41: \n42: class Phase2ExtractGraph(PipelineBase):\n43:     \"\"\"Phase 2: Entity-Relation extraction from compiled wiki notes.\"\"\"\n44: \n45:     def __init__(self):\nThe above content does NOT show the entire file contents. If you need to view any lines of the file which were not shown to complete your task, call this tool again to view those lines.\n"}

Please note that any changes targeting the original code should remove the line number, colon, and leading space.\n1: \"\"\"\n2: core/hybrid_retriever.py — Hybrid RAG Retriever (P3-3)\n3: ======================================================\n4: Vector top-K ∪ GraphStore 1-hop expansion → LLM rerank → ordered context.\n5: \n6: Config (config.yaml):\n7:     hybrid_retriever:\n8:       vector_top_k: 10\n9:       graph_max_hops: 1\n10:       rerank: true\n11:       rerank_model: \"qwen3:8b\"\n12:       rerank_top_n: 5\n13:     vector_db:\n14:       path: \"skills/telegram_kb_agent/state/chroma_db\"\n15: \n16: Requirements:\n17:     pip install networkx chromadb\n18: \"\"\"\n19: \n20: from __future__ import annotations\n21: \n22: import logging\n23: import os\n24: import re\n25: from typing import Any, Dict, List, Optional\n26: \n27: _logger = logging.getLogger(\"OpenClaw.HybridRetriever\")\n28: \n29: \n30: class HybridRetriever:\n31:     \"\"\"Vector top-K ∪ Graph 1-hop → optional LLM rerank.\"\"\"\n32: \n33:     def __init__(self, workspace_root: str, skill_name: str = \"telegram_kb_agent\"):\n34:         self.workspace_root = workspace_root\n35:         self.skill_name = skill_name\n36:         self._chroma_client: Any = None\n37:         self._collection: Any = None\n38:         self._graph_store: Any = None\n39:         self._llm: Any = None\n40: \n41:         from core.config.config_manager import ConfigManager  # type: ignore[import]\n42: \n43:         cfg = ConfigManager(workspace_root, skill_name)\n44:         hr = cfg.get_section(\"hybrid_retriever\") or {}\n45:         self._vector_top_k: int = hr.get(\"vector_top_k\", 10)\n46:         self._graph_max_hops: int = hr.get(\"graph_max_hops\", 1)\n47:         self.\n<truncated 1166 bytes>\n from exc\n69:         if not os.path.exists(self._vector_db_path):\n70:             raise FileNotFoundError(f\"ChromaDB not found at {self._vector_db_path}\")\n71:         self._chroma_client = chromadb.PersistentClient(path=self._vector_db_path)\n72:         self._collection = self._chroma_client.get_collection(\"wiki_knowledge\")\n73:         return self._collection\n74: \n75:     def _get_graph(self):\n76:         if self._graph_store is None:\n77:             from core.ai.graph_store import get_graph_store  # type: ignore[import]\n78: \n79:             self._graph_store = get_graph_store(self.workspace_root, self.skill_name)\n80:         return self._graph_store\n81: \n82:     def _get_llm(self):\n83:         if self._llm is None:\n84:             from core.ai.llm_client import OllamaClient  # type: ignore[import]\n85: \n86:             self._llm = OllamaClient(api_url=self._ollama_url)\n87:         return self._llm\n88: \n89:     def _embed(self, text: str) -> List[float]:\n90:         import requests\n91: \n92:         # Build embeddings URL: strip /generate suffix, append /embeddings\n93:         base_url = self._ollama_url\n94:         for _sfx in (\"/generate\", \"/api/generate\"):\n95:             if base_url.endswith(_sfx):\n96:                 base_url = base_url[: -len(_sfx)]\n97:                 break\n98:         embed_url = base_url.rstrip(\"/\") + \"/embeddings\"\n99: \n100:         resp = requests.post(\n101:             embed_url,\n102:             json={\"model\": self._embed_model, \"prompt\": text},\n103:             timeout=30,\n104:         )\n105:         return resp.json().get(\"embedding\", [0.1] * 768) if resp.ok else [0.1] * 768\n106: \n107:     # Please note that any changes targeting the original code should remove the line number, colon, and leading space.\n1: \"\"\"\n2: core/graph_store.py — Knowledge Graph Abstraction Layer (P3-1)\n3: =============================================================\n4: Provides a unified GraphStore interface over two backends:\n5:   - NetworkX  (local / dev / test — zero external dependencies beyond networkx)\n6:   - Neo4j     (production — requires neo4j Python driver + a running Neo4j instance)\n7: \n8: The active backend is selected at runtime from config.yaml:\n9:     graph:\n10:       backend: \"networkx\"   # or \"neo4j\"\n11:       neo4j:\n12:         uri: \"bolt://localhost:7687\"\n13:         user: \"neo4j\"\n14:         password: \"password\"\n15:       persist_path: \"state/graph.gpickle\"   # for NetworkX backend\n16: \n17: Usage:\n18:     from core.ai.graph_store import get_graph_store\n19:     gs = get_graph_store(workspace_root, skill_name=\"knowledge_compiler\")\n20: \n21:     gs.upsert_entity(\"認知心理學\", labels=[\"Concept\", \"Psychology\"], props={\"source\": \"notes.md\"})\n22:     gs.upsert_relation(\"認知心理學\", \"RELATED_TO\", \"工作記憶\")\n23:     neighbours = gs.get_neighbours(\"認知心理學\", max_hops=1)\n24:     # [{\"name\": \"工作記憶\", \"relation\": \"RELATED_TO\"}, ...]\n25: \n26: Requirements (install only what you need):\n27:     pip install networkx                      # always safe\n28:     pip install neo4j                         # only for production backend\n29: \"\"\"\n30: \n31: from __future__ import annotations\n32: \n33: import logging\n34: import os\n35: import threading\n36: from typing import Any, Dict, List, Optional, Protocol, runtime_checkable\n37: \n38: _logger = loggi\n<truncated 3021 bytes>\n from networkx.readwrite import json_graph\n112: \n113:             from core.utils.atomic_writer import AtomicWriter\n114: \n115:             data = json_graph.node_link_data(self._G)\n116:             AtomicWriter.write_json(self._persist_path, data)\n117:         except Exception as exc:\n118:             _logger.warning(\"[GraphStore/NX] Failed to persist graph: %s\", exc)\n119: \n120:     # ── Interface ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────\n121: \n122:     def upsert_entity(\n123:         self, name: str, labels: List[str], props: Optional[Dict[str, Any]] = None\n124:     ) -> None:\n125:         with self._lock:\n126:             attrs = dict(props or {})\n127:             attrs[\"labels\"] = labels\n128:             if self._G.has_node(name):\n129:                 self._G.nodes[name].update(attrs)\n130:             else:\n131:                 self._G.add_node(name, **attrs)\n132:             self._persist()\n133: \n134:     def upsert_relation(\n135:         self, src: str, rel: str, dst: str, props: Optional[Dict[str, Any]] = None\n136:     ) -> None:\n137:         with self._lock:\n138:             # Auto-create nodes if they don't exist\n139:             for node in (src, dst):\n140:                 if not self._G.has_node(node):\n141:                     self._G.add_node(node, labels=[\"Entity\"])\n142:             attrs = dict(props or {})\n143:             attrs[\"rel\"] = rel\n144:             self._G.add_edge(src, dst, **attrs)\n145:             self._persist()\n146: \n147:     def get_neighbours(self, name: str, max_hops: int = 1) -> List[Dict[str, Any]]:\n148:         \"\"\"Return all nodes reachable within max_hops, with their relation labels.\"\"\"\n149:         with self._lock:\n150:             if not self._G.has_node(name):\nThe above content does NOT show the entire file contents. {"step_index":894,"source":"MODEL","type":"VIEW_FILE","status":"DONE","created_at":"2026-05-29T06:52:42Z","content":"Created At: 2026-05-29T06:52:42Z\nCompleted At: 2026-05-29T06:52:42Z\nFile Path: `file:///Users/limchinkun/Desktop/local-workspace/openclaw-sandbox/core/ai/graph_store.py`\nTotal Lines: 309\nTotal Bytes: 11910\nShowing lines 200 to 309\nThe following code has been modified to include a line number before every line, in the format: <line_number>: <original_line>. Please note that any changes targeting the original code should remove the line number, colon, and leading space.\n200: # ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n201: # Neo4j Backend  (production)\n202: # ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n203: \n204: \n205: class Neo4jGraphStore:\n206:     \"\"\"Production graph backed by a Neo4j Bolt connection.\n207: \n208:     Requires: pip install neo4j\n209:     Configure in config.yaml:\n210:         graph:\n211:           backend: neo4j\n212:           neo4j:\n213:             uri: \"bolt://localhost:7687\"\n214:             user: \"neo4j\"\n215:             password: \"your_password\"\n216:     \"\"\"\n217: \n218:     def __init__(self, uri: str, user: str, password: str):\n219:         try:\n220:             from neo4j import GraphDatabase  # type: ignore[import]\n221: \n222:             self._driver = GraphDatabase.driver(uri, auth=(user, password))\n223:         except ImportError as exc:\n224:             raise ImportError(\n225:                 \"neo4j driver is required for the production GraphStore backend. \"\n226:                 \"Run: pip install neo4j\"\n227:             ) from exc\n228:         _logger.info(\"[GraphStore/Neo4j] Connected to %s\", uri)\n229: \n230:     def upsert_entity(\n231:         self, name: str, labels: List[str], props: Optional[Dict[str, Any]] = None\n232:     ) -> None:\n233:         label_str = \":\".join(labels) if labels else \"Entity\"\n234:         cypher = f\"MERGE (n:{label_str} {{name: $name}}) SET n += $props\"\n235:         with self._driver.session() as s:\n236:             s.run(cypher, name=name, props=props or {})\n237: \n238:     def upsert_relation(\n\n<truncated 1255 bytes>\n 0\n265: \n266:     def close(self) -> None:\n267:         self._driver.close()\n268:         _logger.info(\"[GraphStore/Neo4j] Connection closed.\")\n269: \n270: \n271: # ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n272: # Factory\n273: # ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n274: \n275: \n276: def get_graph_store(workspace_root: str, skill_name: str = \"knowledge_compiler\") -> Any:\n277:     \"\"\"Instantiate and return the configured GraphStore backend.\n278: \n279:     Reads config from skills/<skill_name>/config/config.yaml under the\n280:     ``graph:`` section. Falls back to NetworkX if no config is present.\n281: \n282:     Args:\n283:         workspace_root: Absolute path to the sandbox root.\n284:         skill_name:     Skill whose config.yaml is consulted.\n285: \n286:     Returns:\n287:         A GraphStore-compatible instance (NetworkXGraphStore or Neo4jGraphStore).\n288:     \"\"\"\n289:     from core.config.config_manager import ConfigManager  # type: ignore[import]\n290: \n291:     cfg_mgr = ConfigManager(workspace_root, skill_name)\n292:     graph_cfg: Dict[str, Any] = cfg_mgr.get_section(\"graph\") or {}\n293:     backend = graph_cfg.get(\"backend\", \"networkx\").lower()\n294: \n295:     if backend == \"neo4j\":\n296:         neo_cfg = graph_cfg.get(\"neo4j\", {})\n297:         return Neo4jGraphStore(\n298:             uri=neo_cfg.get(\"uri\", \"bolt://localhost:7687\"),\n299:             user=neo_cfg.get(\"user\", \"neo4j\"),\n300:             password=os.environ.get(\"NEO4J_PASSWORD\", neo_cfg.get(\"password\", \"\")),\n301:         )\n302: \n303:     # Default: NetworkX\n304:     persist_path = graph_cfg.get(\n305:         \"persist_path\",\n306:         os.path.join(workspace_root, \"skills\", skill_name, \"data\", \"state\", \"graph.json\"),\n307:     )\n308:     return NetworkXGraphStore(persist_path=persist_path)\n309: \nThe above content does NOT show the entire file contents. If you need to view any lines of the file which were not shown to complete your task, call this tool again to view those lines.\n"}
{"step_index":895,"source":"MODEL","type":"PLANNER_RESPONSE","status":"DONE","created_at":"2026-05-29T06:52:42Z","thinking":"**Clarifying Data Storage**\n\nI've been examining the `persist_path` for the `NetworkXGraphStore`. Initially, I identified it as a JSON file, but then I found it was a gpickle in `config/config.yaml`. The inconsistency needs further investigation. It seems to resolve through the config/state setup, but this needs confirmation to determine which approach is actually being used.\n\n\n**Confirming File Paths**\n\nI've traced the `persist_path` resolution in detail now, and it seems the relative path \"state/graph.gpickle\" in the config file is resolved relative to the working directory. The working directory, which contains the `state` folder, is the `openclaw-sandbox/` directory. So the `NetworkXGraphStore` correctly loads the graph from the expected `openclaw-sandbox/state/graph.gpickle` file. I've found an existence of `state` folder! I now realize I have to check the instantiation in `hybrid_retriever.py` `_get_graph`.\n\n\n**Resolving Data Conflict**\n\nI've pinpointed a crucial discrepancy. The `hybrid_retriever` is loading an empty graph due to an incorrect `persist_path` resolution. I've realized the `skill_name` argument causes it to default to a different location than where `knowledge_compiler` saves the graph. Adding a `graph` section to `telegram_kb_agent/config/config.yaml` with the shared path resolves the inconsistency, ensuring both access the correct graph. Now I need to decide the canonical path.\n\n\n","tool_calls":[{"name":"replace_file_content","args":{"AllowMultiple":"false","Description":"\"Add graph database configuration to telegram_kb_agent to point to the shared graph.gpickle\"","EndLine":"24","Instruction":"\"Add the graph section to synchronize graph database path with knowledge_compiler\"","ReplacementContent":"\"runtime:\\n  ollama:\\n    api_url: \\\"http://127.0.0.1:11434/api\\\"\\n\\ngraph:\\n  backend: \\\"networkx\\\"\\n  persist_path: \\\"state/graph.gpickle\\\"\"","StartLine":"21","TargetContent":"\"runtime:\\n  ollama:\\n    api_url: \\\"http://127.0.0.1:11434/api\\\"\"","TargetFile":"\"/Users/limchinkun/Desktop/local-workspace/openclaw-sandbox/skills/telegram_kb_agent/config/config.yaml\"","TargetLintErrorIds":"[]","toolAction":"\"Adding graph configuration to telegram_kb_agent config\"","toolSummary":"\"Modify config.yaml for telegram_kb_agent\""}}]}

{"step_index":903,"source":"MODEL","type":"PLANNER_RESPONSE","status":"DONE","created_at":"2026-05-29T06:52:55Z","thinking":"**Analyzing Graph Retrieval Failure**\n\nI've got the graph loaded with its 76 nodes, which is good. However, the critical issue is that the hybrid retriever is reporting zero passages. This is the main blocker. I'm going to add more logging to the `hybrid_retriever.py` file to understand why it's not finding any passages, and to get more information on the failure.\n\n\n**Debugging Candidate Generation**\n\nOkay, I've got a clearer picture now. I'm focusing on the candidate generation within the `hybrid_retriever.py` file. I'm debugging the regex and node matching. Currently, I'm checking the candidates derived from the question and passage sources. It seems the regex is correctly identifying relevant Chinese terms, such as \"非功能性流程模擬\". However, I've confirmed that the exact node name in the graph includes a colon, such as \"Dummy Test: 非功能性流程模擬\", causing the candidate matching to fail.\n\n\n**Refining Node Matching**\n\nI'm now zeroing in on more robust node matching. I realized the initial regex extracts \"Dummy\" and \"Test\" separately, but the graph requires the full name \"Dummy Test\". I have the `file` property for the node which helps, and the `p[\"source\"]` for the passage which is \"Dummy Test 非功能性流程模擬.md\"! Since it seems like I can directly iterate through the nodes in NetworkX, I can match nodes based on the `file` property. The `base_name` matches can be made more flexible! I need to consider Neo4j backend too! I will either add `find_nodes_by_property` to the `GraphStore`, or iterate in NetworkX, or check for `entity_exists`.\n\n\n**Implementing Metadata Title**\n\nI've realized a direct and elegant solution. By modifying `indexer.py` to include the document title in the vector metadata (using a regex to parse the first H1), I can perfectly link the vector chunks to their corresponding graph nodes. This eliminates fuzzy matching! I've confirmed this is standard practice, and the files indeed have titles. I'm verifying the parsing logic in `indexer.py`, and the addition of `\"title\"` to the metadata is straightforward. Re-indexing is fast, and `hybrid_retriever.py` can be updated to retrieve the title from the metadata. I'm now implementing the code changes to the indexer and retriever.\n\n\n","tool_calls":[{"name":"replace_file_content","args":{"AllowMultiple":"false","Description":"\"Add title extraction and metadata inclusion to indexer.py\"","EndLine":"100","Instruction":"\"Parse document title from first H1 line and add it to the chunk metadata in indexer.py\"","ReplacementContent":"\"    for file_path in md_files:\\n        filename = os.path.basename(file_path)\\n        if filename == \\\"INDEX.md\\\":\\n            continue\\n\\n        with open(file_path, encoding=\\\"utf-8\\\") as f:\\n            content = f.read()\\n\\n        # Extract title (first H1)\\n        title_match = re.search(r\\\"^#\\\\s+(.+)$\\\", content, re.MULTILINE)\\n        title = title_match.group(1).strip() if title_match else os.path.splitext(filename)[0]\\n\\n        chunks = chunk_text(content, chunk_size, overlap)\\n\\n        for i, chunk in enumerate(chunks):\\n            doc_id = f\\\"{filename}_chunk_{i}\\\"\\n\\n            try:\\n                emb = get_embedding(chunk, model, api_url)\\n\\n                doc_ids.append(doc_id)\\n                embeddings.append(emb)\\n                documents.append(chunk)\\n                metadatas.append({\\\"filename\\\": filename, \\\"title\\\": title, \\\"chunk_index\\\": i})\\n\\n                print(f\\\"  ✅ 已處理: {doc_id}\\\")\"","StartLine":"74","TargetContent":"\"    for file_path in md_files:\\n        filename = os.path.basename(file_path)\\n        if filename == \\\"INDEX.md\\\":\\n            continue\\n\\n        with open(file_path, encoding=\\\"utf-8\\\") as f:\\n            content = f.read()\\n\\n        chunks = chunk_text(content, chunk_size, overlap)\\n\\n        for i, chunk in enumerate(chunks):\\n            doc_id = f\\\"{filename}_chunk_{i}\\\"\\n\\n            try:\\n                emb = get_embedding(chunk, model, api_url)\\n\\n                doc_ids.append(doc_id)\\n                embeddings.append(emb)\\n                documents.append(chunk)\\n                metadatas.append({\\\"filename\\\": filename, \\\"chunk_index\\\": i})\\n\\n                print(f\\\"  ✅ 已處理: {doc_id}\\\")\"","TargetFile":"\"/Users/limchinkun/Desktop/local-workspace/openclaw-sandbox/skills/telegram_kb_agent/scripts/indexer.py\"","TargetLintErrorIds":"[]","toolAction":"\"Modifying indexer.py to extract title metadata\"","toolSummary":"\"Modify indexer.py\""}}]}

{"step_index":929,"source":"MODEL","type":"PLANNER_RESPONSE","status":"DONE","created_at":"2026-05-29T06:53:42Z","thinking":"**Analyzing API Endpoint Usage**\n\nI've zeroed in on the direct use of `self.api_url` as the endpoint in line 266, raising immediate questions. The config file `config.yaml` is the source of truth, and so I am now evaluating the value of this variable, and how it is being utilized. I need to trace this back to the configuration to understand the source of the endpoint.\n\n\n**Correcting the API Endpoint**\n\nI've discovered the root cause: `OllamaClient` expects the full endpoint, but it's receiving a base URL. My approach will be to fix the `_get_llm` method by appending `/generate` when necessary, to avoid the 404 error. The logic will check if the URL is missing the necessary endpoint suffix.\n\n\n","tool_calls":[{"name":"replace_file_content","args":{"AllowMultiple":"false","Description":"\"Fix Ollama api_url construction in hybrid_retriever._get_llm\"","EndLine":"88","Instruction":"\"Normalize api_url in _get_llm to ensure it ends with /generate for OllamaClient\"","ReplacementContent":"\"    def _get_llm(self):\\n        if self._llm is None:\\n            from core.ai.llm_client import OllamaClient  # type: ignore[import]\\n\\n            api_url = self._ollama_url\\n            if api_url.endswith(\\\"/api\\\"):\\n                api_url = api_url + \\\"/generate\\\"\\n            elif (\\n                not api_url.endswith(\\\"/generate\\\")\\n                and not api_url.endswith(\\\"/chat/completions\\\")\\n                and \\\"/v1\\\" not in api_url\\n            ):\\n                api_url = api_url.rstrip(\\\"/\\\") + \\\"/api/generate\\\"\\n\\n            self._llm = OllamaClient(api_url=api_url)\\n        return self._llm\"","StartLine":"82","TargetContent":"\"    def _get_llm(self):\\n        if self._llm is None:\\n            from core.ai.llm_client import OllamaClient  # type: ignore[import]\\n\\n            self._llm = OllamaClient(api_url=self._ollama_url)\\n        return self._llm\"","TargetFile":"\"/Users/limchinkun/Desktop/local-workspace/openclaw-sandbox/core/ai/hybrid_retriever.py\"","TargetLintErrorIds":"[]","toolAction":"\"Fixing API URL normalization in hybrid_retriever.py\"","toolSummary":"\"Modify hybrid_retriever.py\""}}]}

{"step_index":940,"source":"MODEL","type":"VIEW_FILE","status":"DONE","created_at":"2026-05-29T07:04:50Z","content":"Created At: 2026-05-29T07:04:50Z\nCompleted At: 2026-05-29T07:04:50Z\nFile Path: `file:///Users/limchinkun/.gemini/antigravity/brain/32729395-ac4d-4d84-8be5-537c18482c2d/walkthrough.md`\nTotal Lines: 30\nTotal Bytes: 2329\nShowing lines 1 to 30\nThe following code has been modified to include a line number before every line, in the format: <line_number>: <original_line>. Please note that any changes targeting the original code should remove the line number, colon, and leading space.\n1: # Phase 4 Code Review Fixes Completion\n2: \n3: This walkthrough details the final checks for the V9.24 Incubator Route Test code review.\n4: \n5: ## 1. Migration of `open(..., \"w\")` to `AtomicWriter`\n6: \n7: We conducted a thorough codebase search for any remaining `open(..., \"w\")` calls across `core/` and `skills/`. The search returned occurrences only in the following contexts:\n8: \n9: - **Lockfiles**: Used alongside `fcntl.flock` (e.g. `core/orchestration/run_all_pipelines.py`, `core/state/state_backend.py`, `core/state/state_manager.py`). These MUST use raw `open()` because atomic writes operate on temporary files which break `fcntl` file descriptor locks.\n10: - **Null Devices**: `with open(os.devnull, \"w\")` in `audio_transcriber` for silencing third-party module outputs.\n11: - **Test / Dev Scripts**: `ops/`, `scripts/`, `tests/`.\n12: \n13: Conclusion: The `AtomicWriter` migration is **fully complete** for production data logic. No further migrations are required.\n14: \n15: ## 2. Universal Handoff / End-to-End Routing Verification\n16: \n17: We verified that the `student_researcher` output successfully routes to `knowledge_compiler`. \n18: \n19: **Findings**:\n20: - When `Phase2Synthesis` writes the final `_research.md` file, it correctly calls `self.emit_completed(...)`.\n21: - `RouterAgent` catches the `PipelineCompleted` domain event on the local EventBus.\n22: - `RouterAgent._on_pipeline_completed` retrieves the `chain` and `subject` metadata, then physically moves the file from `student_researcher/input/` to `knowledge_compiler/input/`.\n23: - We patched the local `scripts/simulate_routing.py` mock `LocalTaskQueue` to strictly verify this without incurring heavy PDF/LLM overhead. The test passed, correctly enqueuing the `knowledge_compiler` pipeline downstream after `student_researcher` finished.\n24: \n25: > [!TIP]\n26: > **Orchestration Note**: Handoffs relying on `EventBus` require `RouterAgent` and the pipelines to operate within the same Python process/Daemon (e.g., via `inbox_daemon.py` or the `LocalTaskQueue` daemon worker). Standalone script invocations (e.g., manual `uv run skills/student_researcher/scripts/run_all.py`) do not share the in-memory EventBus with `RouterAgent`, meaning automatic handoff will not occur under manual CLI execution. \n27: \n28: ## Final Status\n29: All tasks on the V9.24 Incubator Route Code Review checklist (`task.md`) are now successfully implemented and verified.\n30: \nThe above content shows the entire, complete file contents of the requested file.\n"}

I'm leaning toward a unified, integrated approach.\n\n\n","tool_calls":[{"name":"write_to_file","args":{"ArtifactMetadata":"{\"ArtifactType\":\"walkthrough\",\"RequestFeedback\":false,\"Summary\":\"Detailed walkthrough of Phase B Memory & Graph RAG implementation and testing results\"}","CodeContent":"\"# Walkthrough — Phase B: Memory & Graph RAG Deep Integration\\n\\nThis walkthrough details the design, implementation, and verification of the Vector-seeded Graph Expansion strategy in `HybridRetriever`.\\n\\n## 1. Summary of Changes\\n\\nWe implemented a deep integration between the Vector DB (`ChromaDB`) and the Graph Store (`NetworkX`) to replace the fragile regex-based keyword extraction logic.\\n\\n### Core Architecture Updates\\n\\n1. **`core/ai/hybrid_retriever.py`**:\\n   - Upgraded the query logic to extract primary graph seed candidates from the retrieved Vector passages' metadata (document `title` and `filename` base), in addition to the regex keyword fallback from the raw question.\\n   - Fixed a configuration mismatch where `HybridRetriever` fell back to a sandbox-level default database path (`skills/telegram_kb_agent/state/chroma_db`) instead of the correct project data directory (`data/telegram_kb_agent/state/chroma_db`).\\n   - Standardized LLM API URL parsing to dynamically correct and append `/generate` endpoints for the `OllamaClient` when base paths are configured.\\n\\n2. **`skills/telegram_kb_agent/scripts/indexer.py`**:\\n   - Added automatic document H1-header extraction to attach the true `title` (which serves as the Node Name in the Graph Store) to the metadata of each chunk written to ChromaDB.\\n   - Imported the missing `re` module required for H1 regex parsing.\\n\\n3. **`skills/knowledge_compiler/scripts/run_all.py`**:\\n   - Unified Phase 1 (Compile) and Phase 2 (Graph Extract) into a single orchestrator run so that compiling markdown files automatically refreshes the Entity-Relation graph.\\n   - Refactored `p01_compile.py` and `p02_extract_graph.py` to correctly fall back to the project-relative `data/wiki` directory when `wiki_dir` in `config.yaml` is specified as an empty string `\\\"\\\"` (preventing workspace root pollution).\\n\\n---\\n\\n## 2. Verification Results\\n\\nWe verified the pipeline from note generation to hybrid RAG query using a series of local integration runs.\\n\\n### Step 2.1: Note Synt\n<truncated 2242 bytes>","Description":"\"Create walkthrough for Phase B Memory & Graph RAG Deep Integration\"","IsArtifact":"true","Overwrite":"true","TargetFile":"\"/Users/limchinkun/.gemini/antigravity/brain/32729395-ac4d-4d84-8be5-537c18482c2d/walkthrough.md\"","toolAction":"\"Writing Phase B walkthrough to walkthrough.md\"","toolSummary":"\"Write walkthrough.md\""}}]}

{"step_index":950,"source":"MODEL","type":"PLANNER_RESPONSE","status":"DONE","created_at":"2026-05-29T07:18:18Z","content":"我已成功完成 Phase B 的所有開發、配置修正與端到端驗證任務！\n\n### 📋 任務狀態更新\n\n所有任務已順利執行完畢並在任務清單中標記為完成。詳細的實作細節、配置修復以及實際的檢索測試結果，請參閱以下產出的 Artifact：\n\n- **任務清單**：[task.md](file:///Users/limchinkun/.gemini/antigravity/brain/32729395-ac4d-4d84-8be5-537c18482c2d/task.md)\n- **實作與驗證成果紀錄**：[walkthrough.md](file:///Users/limchinkun/.gemini/antigravity/brain/32729395-ac4d-4d84-8be5-537c18482c2d/walkthrough.md)\n\n### 💡 核心成果摘要\n\n1. **修正路徑與載入邏輯**：\n   - 解決了 `knowledge_compiler` 對 `wiki_dir` 為空字串 `\"\"` 時解析為當前工作目錄（造成專案根目錄污染）的 Bug，正確回退至 `data/wiki`。\n   - 修正了 `hybrid_retriever.py` 預設 ChromaDB 路徑與 `indexer.py` 不一致的問題，並同步了 `telegram_kb_agent` 的 GraphStore 儲存路徑。\n   - 修復了 Rerank 呼叫 OllamaClient 時因 `api_url` 缺少 `/generate` 後綴導致 404 的問題。\n\n2. **雙源檢索深度整合（Vector-seeded Graph Expansion）**：\n   - 升級了 `indexer.py`，使每個寫入 ChromaDB 的知識 Chunk 都包含其 H1 標題（對應圖譜中的實體節點名稱）。\n   - 在 `hybrid_retriever.py` 中，將 Vector 檢索到的 Top-K 文檔實體（標題/檔名）與問題分詞 candidates 合併為 Seed，以 1-hop 深度擴展圖譜鄰居，實現精準關聯。\n\n3. **端到端檢索驗證**：\n   - 經由 `scratch/test_hybrid.py` 驗證，當前已能成功取得「圖譜關係資料（如 `IS_A` 關係）」與「向量原始段落文字」，並順利將其整合交給 LLM 重排（Rerank），回傳最相關的雙源脈絡（Vector + Graph）給下游 Q&A！"}

Please note that any changes targeting the original code should remove the line number, colon, and leading space.\\\\n1: # System Context \\u0026 Development Contract\\\\n2: \\\\n3: \\u003e **Target Audience:** Development AIs (Google Antigravity, Claude Code, GitHub Copilot)\\\\n4: \\u003e **Purpose:** Defines the active hardware constraints, End-of-Feature protocol, and Documentation update rules for the workspace.\\\\n5: \\u003e **Last Updated:** 2026-05-22\\\\n6: \\\\n7: ## 1. Environment Constraints \\u0026 Toolchain (uv-native)\\\\n8: \\\\n9: - **OS**: macOS Apple Silicon\\\\n10: - **Hardware**: 16 GB RAM (Strict memory limits apply — strictly one heavy model at a time. Watchdog evicts models if memory threshold is exceeded. Heavy VLM models like `llama3.2-vision` (7.8GB) force a strict sequential `Semaphore(1)` execution constraint to prevent OOM and API timeouts).\\\\n11: - **Primary Language**: Code in English, Communication in Traditional Chinese.\\\\n12: -
