I've pinpointed a failure involving `shutil.move` being called, but I am now unsure where it came from. Since `tq.join()` is used in the first test, there are no pending tasks. I'm considering the possibility that a previous test enqueued a failing task, and the background thread is still processing it. The key seems to be figuring out the relationship between failed tasks, retries, and the eventual call to move the file to the quarantine location. The `inbox_daemon` also uses `LocalTaskQueue().enqueue` but is not used in the test.\n\n\n**Clarifying Directory Monitoring**\n\nI'm now investigating how the `InboxDaemon` interacts with the `tmp_workspace` directory across multiple tests. My focus shifted towards understanding if a background thread from a previous test (`LocalTaskQueue`) might still be running and interfering. {"step_index":1220,"source":"MODEL","type":"VIEW_FILE","status":"DONE","created_at":"2026-05-29T09:44:42Z","content":"Created At: 2026-05-29T09:44:42Z\nCompleted At: 2026-05-29T09:44:42Z\nFile Path: `file:///Users/limchinkun/Desktop/local-workspace/openclaw-sandbox/core/orchestration/task_queue.py`\nTotal Lines: 230\nTotal Bytes: 8414\nShowing lines 1 to 230\nThe following code has been modified to include a line number before every line, in the format: <line_number>: <original_line>. Please note that any changes targeting the original code should remove the line number, colon, and leading space.\n1: \"\"\"\n2: core/task_queue.py — Single-Threaded Task Queue for OOM Prevention\n3: ===================================================================\n4: A lightweight, background job queue that ensures Open Claw skills run\n5: sequentially. This replaces the old concurrent `subprocess.Popen` logic\n6: in `inbox_daemon.py` which could easily crash the system (OOM) when\n7: multiple files arrive simultaneously.\n8: \"\"\"\n9: \n10: from datetime import datetime\n11: import json\n12: import os\n13: import queue\n14: import shutil\n15: import subprocess\n16: import threading\n17: import time\n18: from typing import Dict, List, Optional\n19: \n20: from core.orchestration.event_bus import DomainEvent, EventBus\n21: from core.utils.log_manager import build_logger\n22: \n23: # Use workspace root to locate the data directory for quarantine\n24: from core.utils.workspace import get_workspace_root\n25: \n26: _workspace_root = get_workspace_root()\n27: _logger = build_logger(\n28:     \"OpenClaw.TaskQueue\", log_file=os.path.join(_workspace_root, \"logs\", \"task_queue.log\")\n29: )\n30: \n31: \n32: class LocalTaskQueue:\n33:     _instance = None\n34:     _lock = threading.Lock()\n35: \n36:     def __new__(cls):\n37:         with cls._lock:\n38:             if cls._instance is None:\n39:                 cls._instance = super().__new__(cls)\n40:                 cls._instance._init()\n41:             return cls._instance\n42: \n43:     def _init(self):\n44:         self.q = queue.Queue()\n45:         self.max_retries = 3\n46:         self.timeout_sec = 3600  # 1 hour max per pipeline task to prevent hangin\n<truncated 5923 bytes>\n] 達到最大重試次數，放棄任務: %s\", name)\n173:             self._quarantine_file(task, error_msg)\n174:         else:\n175:             backoff_sec = 5 * (2 ** task[\"retry_count\"])\n176:             _logger.warning(\n177:                 \"🔄 [TaskQueue] 準備重試任務 (等待 %ds 後排入佇列尾端): %s\", backoff_sec, name\n178:             )\n179: \n180:             try:\n181:                 from core.services.telegram_bot import send_message\n182: \n183:                 send_message(\n184:                     error_msg\n185:                     + f\"\\n將於 {backoff_sec} 秒後進行第 {task['retry_count'] + 1} 次重試...\"\n186:                 )\n187:             except ImportError:\n188:                 pass\n189: \n190:             time.sleep(backoff_sec)\n191:             self.q.put(task)\n192: \n193:     def enqueue(\n194:         self,\n195:         name: str,\n196:         cmd: List[str],\n197:         cwd: str,\n198:         filepath: Optional[str] = None,\n199:         skill: Optional[str] = None,\n200:         chain: Optional[List[str]] = None,\n201:         subject: str = \"Default\",\n202:         env: Optional[Dict[str, str]] = None,\n203:     ):\n204:         \"\"\"\n205:         Add a task to the queue.\n206:         \"\"\"\n207:         self.q.put(\n208:             {\n209:                 \"name\": name,\n210:                 \"cmd\": cmd,\n211:                 \"cwd\": cwd,\n212:                 \"filepath\": filepath,\n213:                 \"skill\": skill,\n214:                 \"chain\": chain or [],\n215:                 \"subject\": subject,\n216:                 \"env\": env,\n217:                 \"retry_count\": 0,\n218:             }\n219:         )\n220: \n221:         _logger.info(\"📥 [TaskQueue] 任務已排入佇列 (等候中: %d): %s\", self.q.qsize(), name)\n222: \n223:     def join(self):\n224:         \"\"\"Block until all tasks are completed.\"\"\"\n225:         self.q.join()\n226: \n227: \n228: # Global instance\n229: task_queue = LocalTaskQueue()\n230: \nThe above content shows the entire, complete file contents of the requested file.\n"}

Create a dummy file inside raw_path so path routing works\n11:     inbox_dir = os.path.join(daemon.raw_path, \"Default\")\n12:     os.makedirs(inbox_dir, exist_ok=True)\n13:     test_file = os.path.join(inbox_dir, \"test.pdf\")\n14:     with open(test_file, \"w\") as f:\n15:         f.write(\"dummy content\")\n16: \n17:     with (\n18:         patch.object(daemon.stability_poller, \"schedule_trigger\") as mock_schedule,\n19:         patch(\"core.services.inbox_daemon.os.rename\"),\n20:         patch(\"core.services.inbox_daemon.update_session_manifest\"),\n21:     ):\n22:         # {"step_index":1227,"source":"MODEL","type":"VIEW_FILE","status":"DONE","created_at":"2026-05-29T09:44:58Z","content":"Created At: 2026-05-29T09:44:58Z\nCompleted At: 2026-05-29T09:44:59Z\nFile Path: `file:///Users/limchinkun/Desktop/local-workspace/openclaw-sandbox/core/services/inbox_daemon.py`\nTotal Lines: 391\nTotal Bytes: 15531\nShowing lines 1 to 391\nThe following code has been modified to include a line number before every line, in the format: <line_number>: <original_line>. Please note that any changes targeting the original code should remove the line number, colon, and leading space.\n1: \"\"\"\n2: inbox_daemon.py — Open Claw System-Wide Inbox Monitor\n3: ======================================================\n4: Monitors the `inbox` canonical directories for all active skills using Watchdog.\n5: When a new file arrives (e.g. .m4a for audio_transcriber, .pdf for doc_parser),\n6: it triggers the target skill's pipeline via the WebUI API (with direct subprocess\n7: fallback when the WebUI is not running).\n8: \n9: Design:\n10:   - Watches the global `data/raw` directory.\n11:   - Routes files to specific skills based on extension (.m4a -> voice-memo, .pdf -> pdf-knowledge).\n12:   - File stability via size-polling (2s interval) with 300s timeout guard.\n13:   - Same-file debounce via threading.Event (cancellable).\n14: \"\"\"\n15: \n16: import json\n17: import logging\n18: import os\n19: import re\n20: import sys\n21: import threading\n22: import time\n23: from typing import Any, Optional\n24: \n25: # Path Resolution MUST happen before importing internal packages\n26: _core_dir = os.path.dirname(os.path.abspath(__file__))\n27: from core.utils.workspace import get_workspace_root\n28: \n29: _workspace_root = get_workspace_root()\n30: \n31: from core.cli.cli_runner import SkillRunner\n32: from core.orchestration.router_agent import RouterAgent, TaskManifest\n33: from core.orchestration.session_manifest import update_session_manifest\n34: from core.orchestration.skill_registry import SkillRegistry\n35: from core.utils.atomic_writer import AtomicWriter\n36: from core.utils.file_stability import FileStabilityPoller\n37: \n38: _logger = logging.getLogger(\"OpenClaw.InboxDaem\n<truncated 13843 bytes>\n: remaining_chain,\n338:                         \"subject\": subject,\n339:                         \"model\": env.get(\"OPENCLAW_ROUTER_MODEL\") if env else None,\n340:                     },\n341:                 )\n342:             )\n343: \n344:             # Remove the pending file so we don't trigger it again\n345:             os.remove(pending_path)\n346: \n347:             # File stability poller already debounces this, no need for manual lock\n348: \n349:         except Exception as e:\n350:             _logger.error(\n351:                 \"_check_proofreader_resume 發生未預期錯誤: %s — %s\", filepath, e, exc_info=True\n352:             )\n353: \n354:     def stop(self) -> None:\n355:         \"\"\"Gracefully stop the observer and all pending poll threads.\"\"\"\n356:         if self._observer:\n357:             self._observer.stop()\n358:             self._observer.join()\n359:             _logger.info(\"監控已停止\")\n360:         with self._lock:\n361:             self._seen_files.clear()\n362: \n363: \n364: if __name__ == \"__main__\":\n365:     import argparse\n366: \n367:     logging.basicConfig(\n368:         level=logging.INFO,\n369:         format=\"%(asctime)s [%(name)s] %(levelname)s %(message)s\",\n370:         datefmt=\"%Y-%m-%d %H:%M:%S\",\n371:     )\n372: \n373:     parser = argparse.ArgumentParser(description=\"Open Claw Inbox Daemon\")\n374:     parser.add_argument(\n375:         \"--scan-only\", action=\"store_true\", help=\"僅手動掃描並歸檔現有檔案，不啟動常駐監控\"\n376:     )\n377:     args = parser.parse_args()\n378: \n379:     daemon = SystemInboxDaemon()\n380:     if args.scan_only:\n381:         _logger.info(\"執行手動歸檔模式 (Scan Only)...\")\n382:         daemon.scan_all()\n383:     else:\n384:         daemon.start()\n385:         daemon.scan_all()  # Initial scan on startup\n386:         try:\n387:             while True:\n388:                 time.sleep(1)\n389:         except KeyboardInterrupt:\n390:             daemon.stop()\n391: \nThe above content shows the entire, complete file contents of the requested file.\n"}

{"step_index":1230,"source":"MODEL","type":"VIEW_FILE","status":"DONE","created_at":"2026-05-29T09:45:07Z","content":"Created At: 2026-05-29T09:45:07Z\nCompleted At: 2026-05-29T09:45:07Z\nFile Path: `file:///Users/limchinkun/Desktop/local-workspace/openclaw-sandbox/core/utils/file_stability.py`\nTotal Lines: 86\nTotal Bytes: 2867\nShowing lines 1 to 86\nThe following code has been modified to include a line number before every line, in the format: <line_number>: <original_line>. Please note that any changes targeting the original code should remove the line number, colon, and leading space.\n1: \"\"\"\n2: core/utils/file_stability.py\n3: ============================\n4: Provides FileStabilityPoller for debouncing file arrival events.\n5: Monitors a file's size until it remains unchanged for a specified interval,\n6: then triggers a callback.\n7: \"\"\"\n8: \n9: from __future__ import annotations\n10: \n11: import logging\n12: import os\n13: import threading\n14: import time\n15: from typing import Callable\n16: \n17: _logger = logging.getLogger(\"OpenClaw.FileStability\")\n18: \n19: \n20: class FileStabilityPoller:\n21:     \"\"\"\n22:     Debounces file arrival/modification events by polling file size.\n23:     Triggers the callback only when the file size is stable (unchanged)\n24:     over a poll interval and > 0 bytes.\n25:     \"\"\"\n26: \n27:     def __init__(self, poll_interval_sec: float = 2.0, timeout_sec: float = 600.0):\n28:         self.poll_interval_sec = poll_interval_sec\n29:         self.timeout_sec = timeout_sec\n30:         self._lock = threading.RLock()\n31:         self._debounce_events: dict[str, threading.Event] = {}\n32: \n33:     def schedule_trigger(self, filepath: str, callback: Callable[[str], None]) -> None:\n34:         \"\"\"\n35:         Cancel any existing poll thread for this filepath,\n36:         then start a fresh one with its own stop Event.\n37:         \"\"\"\n38:         with self._lock:\n39:             existing_event = self._debounce_events.get(filepath)\n40:             if existing_event:\n41:                 existing_event.set()\n42:             stop_event = threading.Event()\n43:             self._debounce_events[filepath] = stop_event\n44: \n45:         t = threading.Thread(\n46:             target=self._wait_and_trigger,\n47:             args=(filepath, stop_event, callback),\n48:             daemon=True,\n49:         )\n50:         t.start()\n51: \n52:     def _wait_and_trigger(\n53:         self, filepath: str, stop_event: threading.Event, callback: Callable[[str], None]\n54:     ) -> None:\n55:         prev_size = -1\n56:         elapsed = 0.0\n57: \n58:         while elapsed < self.timeout_sec:\n59:             if stop_event.is_set():\n60:                 _logger.debug(\"去抖取消，停止等待: %s\", filepath)\n61:                 return\n62: \n63:             try:\n64:                 if not os.path.exists(filepath):\n65:                     _logger.warning(\"檔案已消失，放棄觸發: %s\", filepath)\n66:                     return\n67:                 current_size = os.path.getsize(filepath)\n68:                 if current_size == prev_size and current_size > 0:\n69:                     break  # Stable!\n70:                 prev_size = current_size\n71:             except OSError as e:\n72:                 _logger.debug(\"輪詢檔案大小時發生 OSError: %s — %s\", filepath, e)\n73: \n74:             time.sleep(self.poll_interval_sec)\n75:             elapsed += self.poll_interval_sec\n76:         else:\n77:             _logger.error(\"等待超時 (%ss)，放棄觸發: %s\", self.timeout_sec, filepath)\n78:             return\n79: \n80:         # Clean up debounce map\n81:         with self._lock:\n82:             if self._debounce_events.get(filepath) is stop_event:\n83:                 self._debounce_events.pop(filepath, None)\n84: \n85:         callback(filepath)\n86: \nThe above content shows the entire, complete file contents of the requested file.\n"}

I'm now certain the original thread remains active, although seemingly idle.\n\n\n**Investigating Retry Mechanism**\n\nI'm now diving deep into the retry mechanism to understand why `shutil.move` was called twice. I've pinpointed that `mock_run.call_count` indicated multiple failures, triggering the retry logic in `_handle_failure`. The key is that `time.sleep` delays re-enqueueing the task, causing the multiple moves. I'm scrutinizing `_worker_loop` and `_handle_failure` to clarify the flow. I'm now certain `tq.join()` is blocked, but retry continues independently, re-enqueueing the failed task.\n\n\n**Unraveling Task Completion**\n\nI'm now zeroing in on the task completion process within the worker loop. My understanding is that `task_done()` is called on the *original* task, even after retries, causing the queue's unfinished task count to misrepresent the retry status. I've confirmed that the queue count will not reach zero until the final retry calls `_quarantine_file` because that task is not re-enqueued. This finally unblocks `tq.join()`. If multiple calls to `shutil.move` are observed, it suggests either excessive failures or potentially the presence of an unexpected second task within the queue, which is counter-intuitive and unexpected. I'm investigating why a second task might be present.\n\n\n**Tracing Thread Initialization**\n\nI'm now investigating the thread start process when `schedule_trigger` is called. {"step_index":1239,"source":"MODEL","type":"RUN_COMMAND","status":"DONE","created_at":"2026-05-29T09:46:03Z","content":"Created At: 2026-05-29T09:46:03Z\nCompleted At: 2026-05-29T09:46:05Z\n\n\t\t\t\tThe command completed successfully.\n\t\t\t\tOutput:\n\t\t\t\timport os\nfrom unittest.mock import MagicMock, patch\n\nfrom core.orchestration.task_queue import LocalTaskQueue\n\n\n@patch(\"core.orchestration.task_queue.subprocess.run\")\ndef test_task_queue_success(mock_run, tmp_workspace):\n    mock_run.return_value = MagicMock(returncode=0)\n\n    tq = LocalTaskQueue()\n    # Replace worker thread to avoid actually starting a background thread for tests\n    # We will manually call the loop body or test logic.\n    # Actually, we can just push a task and let the thread run, then join.\n\n    tq.enqueue(\"Test Task\", [\"echo\", \"test\"], tmp_workspace)\n\n    # Wait for processing\n    tq.join()\n\n    # Verify subprocess was called\n    assert mock_run.call_count >= 1\n\n\n@patch(\"core.orchestration.task_queue.subprocess.run\")\n@patch(\"core.orchestration.task_queue.shutil.move\")\ndef test_task_queue_dlq_quarantine(mock_move, mock_run, tmp_workspace):\n    # Simulate a process that always fails\n    mock_run.return_value = MagicMock(returncode=1)\n\n    tq = LocalTaskQueue()\n    # We want to test max retries.\n    # To prevent test from hanging, we'll enqueue a task and wait for it to be processed max_retries times.\n\n    dummy_file = os.path.join(tmp_workspace, \"dummy.pdf\")\n    with open(dummy_file, \"w\") as f:\n        f.write(\"test\")\n\n    tq.enqueue(\"Fail Task\", [\"false\"], tmp_workspace, filepath=dummy_file, skill=\"test-skill\")\n\n    tq.join()\n\n    # Since it fails 3 times, run should be called 3 times.\n    # Note: Because we start the thread in __init__, it might race.\n    # But since we use .join(), it blocks until the queue is empty (meaning all retries are done and it's quarantined).\n    assert mock_run.call_count >= 3\n\n    # Verify it was quarantined\n    print(mock_move.call_args_list); assert mock_move.call_count == 1\n    args, _ = mock_move.call_args\n    assert args[0] == dummy_file\n    assert \"quarantine\" in args[1]\n\n"}

Please note that any changes targeting the original code should remove the line number, colon, and leading space.\n1: \"\"\"\n2: core/skill_registry.py — Dynamic Plugin Discovery Architecture (#17)\n3: ====================================================================\n4: Enables zero-friction extensibility: new Skills are auto-discovered\n5: by scanning the skills/ directory at startup for a manifest.py file\n6: that conforms to the SkillManifest protocol.\n7: \n8: CURRENT STATUS: Fully functional discovery + registration.\n9:   Async execution wiring (#16) and CLI/Telegram auto-population\n10:   are the remaining P3 integration tasks.\n11: \n12: Usage:\n13:     from core.orchestration.skill_registry import SkillRegistry\n14: \n15:     registry = SkillRegistry(skills_root=\"/path/to/skills\")\n16:     registry.discover()\n17: \n18:     for name, manifest in registry.all().items():\n19:         logger.info(f\"{name}: {manifest.description}\")\n20: \n21:     run_fn = registry.get_run_fn(\"audio_transcriber\")\n22:     run_fn(subject=\"math\", force=False)\n23: \"\"\"\n24: \n25: from __future__ import annotations\n26: \n27: from dataclasses import dataclass, field\n28: import importlib.util\n29: import os\n30: import sys\n31: from typing import Callable, Dict, List, Optional\n32: \n33: import logging\n34: \n35: logger = logging.getLogger(\"OpenClaw.skill_registry\")\n36: \n37: # ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n38: # SkillManifest Protocol (#17)\n39: # ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n40: \n41: \n42: @dataclass\n43: class SkillManifest:\n44:     \"\"\"Interface contract every discoverable Skill must implement.\n45: \n46:     \n<truncated 5789 bytes>\nore[union-attr]\n167:         finally:\n168:             sys.path.remove(skill_dir)\n169: \n170:         manifest = getattr(mod, \"MANIFEST\", None)\n171:         if not isinstance(manifest, SkillManifest):\n172:             raise TypeError(\n173:                 f\"manifest.py must expose MANIFEST: SkillManifest, got {type(manifest)}\"\n174:             )\n175:         return manifest\n176: \n177:     def register(self, manifest: SkillManifest) -> None:\n178:         \"\"\"Manually register a SkillManifest (for testing or programmatic use).\"\"\"\n179:         self._registry[manifest.skill_name] = manifest\n180: \n181:     def get(self, skill_name: str) -> Optional[SkillManifest]:\n182:         \"\"\"Return a SkillManifest by name, or None if not found.\"\"\"\n183:         return self._registry.get(skill_name)\n184: \n185:     def get_run_fn(self, skill_name: str) -> Callable:\n186:         \"\"\"Return the run callable for a skill, raising KeyError if missing.\"\"\"\n187:         manifest = self._registry.get(skill_name)\n188:         if manifest is None:\n189:             raise KeyError(\n190:                 f\"Skill '{skill_name}' not registered. Run SkillRegistry.discover() first.\"\n191:             )\n192:         if manifest.run_fn is None:\n193:             raise NotImplementedError(\n194:                 f\"Skill '{skill_name}' has no run_fn defined in its manifest.\"\n195:             )\n196:         return manifest.run_fn\n197: \n198:     def all(self) -> Dict[str, SkillManifest]:\n199:         \"\"\"Return a copy of the full registry.\"\"\"\n200:         return dict(self._registry)\n201: \n202:     def list_names(self) -> List[str]:\n203:         \"\"\"Return sorted list of all registered skill names.\"\"\"\n204:         return sorted(self._registry.keys())\n205: \n206:     def for_file_type(self, ext: str) -> List[SkillManifest]:\n207:         \"\"\"Return skills that can handle a given file extension.\"\"\"\n208:         return [m for m in self._registry.values() if ext.lower() in m.file_types]\n209: \nThe above content shows the entire, complete file contents of the requested file.\n"}

{"step_index":1272,"source":"MODEL","type":"VIEW_FILE","status":"DONE","created_at":"2026-05-29T09:48:38Z","content":"Created At: 2026-05-29T09:48:38Z\nCompleted At: 2026-05-29T09:48:38Z\nFile Path: `file:///Users/limchinkun/Desktop/local-workspace/openclaw-sandbox/skills/audio_transcriber/SKILL.md`\nTotal Lines: 79\nTotal Bytes: 3026\nShowing lines 1 to 79\nThe following code has been modified to include a line number before every line, in the format: <line_number>: <original_line>. Please note that any changes targeting the original code should remove the line number, colon, and leading space.\n1: ---\n2: name: audio_transcriber\n3: description: End-to-end voice processing pipeline. Converts .m4a audio into polished, MLX-Whisper transcribed Obsidian-ready study notes.\n4: metadata:\n5:   {\n6:     \"openclaw\":\n7:       {\n8:         \"emoji\": \"🎙️\"\n9:       }\n10:   }\n11: state_tracking:\n12:   phases: [\"p1\", \"p2\", \"p3\"]\n13:   labels:\n14:     p1: \"P1 (轉錄)\"\n15:     p2: \"P2 (校對)\"\n16:     p3: \"P3 (合併)\"\n17: io_contracts:\n18:   consumes:\n19:     - \"audio/mp4\"\n20:     - \"audio/mpeg\"\n21:     - \"audio/wav\"\n22:   produces:\n23:     - \"text/markdown\"\n24: ---\n25: \n26: # Audio Transcriber Skill\n27: \n28: > **Pipeline**: M4A → Glossary → Transcription → Proofread → Merge\n29: \n30: ## Quick Start\n31: \n32: '''bash\n33: # 1. Drop audio files into the Universal Inbox\n34: cp lecture.m4a data/raw/<SubjectName>/\n35: \n36: # 2. Run in headless batch mode\n37: python3 skills/audio_transcriber/scripts/run_all.py --process-all\n38: \n39: # 3. Check pipeline progress\n40: cat data/audio_transcriber/state/checklist.md\n41: '''\n42: \n43: ## Anti-Hallucination Defense Architecture (V8.1)\n44: \n45: | Phase | Script | Function |\n46: |:---:|:---|:---|\n47: | P0 | `p00_glossary.py` | Glossary initialisation — prevents LLM from fabricating domain-specific terms |\n48: | P1 | `p01_transcribe.py` | High-fidelity transcription via MLX-Whisper. **VAD silence-trimming guard**: uses `pydub.silence` to pre-strip noise (Requires `ffmpeg` installed on system to decode audio files). If `silence_ratio > max_removal_ratio` (default: 0.90) or ffmpeg decoding fails, automatically falls back to the raw audio to prevent over-trimming. Includes multi-clip majority-vote language detection. |\n49: | P2 | `p02_glossary_apply.py` | Automatically applies glossary constraints to the raw transcript to ensure terminology accuracy before proofreading. |\n50: | P3 | `p03_merge.py` | Cross-segment consolidation and refinement. |\n51: | Handoff | (Autonomous) | The RouterAgent automatically forwards the merged transcript to the `proofreader` skill for AI verification against reference material and dashboard review. |\n52: \n53: ## Common Commands\n54: \n55: '''bash\n56: # Run full pipeline on all pending files\n57: python3 skills/audio_transcriber/scripts/run_all.py --process-all\n58: \n59: # Run from a specific phase\n60: python3 skills/audio_transcriber/scripts/run_all.py --from 2\n61: \n62: # Force re-run on all files\n63: python3 skills/audio_transcriber/scripts/run_all.py --process-all --force\n64: \n65: # Regenerate glossary only\n66: python3 skills/audio_transcriber/scripts/run_all.py --glossary\n67: \n68: # Process a single subject\n69: python3 skills/audio_transcriber/scripts/run_all.py --subject <SubjectName>\n70: '''\n71: \n72: ## Global Standards\n73: \n74: - **Zero Temperature**: `config.yaml` enforces `temperature: 0` to guarantee deterministic, hallucination-free outputs.\n75: - **Headless CLI**: Supports `--process-all`, `--from`, `--force`, `--resume`, `--log-json` for full CI/CD compatibility.\n76: - **Preflight Check**: Validates all dependencies and config on every run before processing begins.\n77: - **Checkpoint Resume**: All phase completions are saved to `state/`; use `--resume` to continue after an interruption.\n78: - macOS native notifications (`osascript`) and graceful `KeyboardInterrupt` handling with checkpoint save.\n79: \nThe above content shows the entire, complete file contents of the requested file.\n"}

system.\n\n\n","tool_calls":[{"name":"write_to_file","args":{"ArtifactMetadata":"{\"ArtifactType\":\"implementation_plan\",\"RequestFeedback\":true,\"Summary\":\"Standardizing DAG dashboard display and rich terminal execution output with progress bars and ETA\"}","CodeContent":"\"# 統一 DAG 面板與終端機執行風格 (UI Standardisation)\\n\\n為了解決目前各個 Skill 的 DAG 面板上名稱 hardcoded，以及終端機執行時印出過多除錯細節且缺乏 ETA 預估時間的問題，我規劃了以下的實作計畫。\\n\\n## 核心目標\\n\\n1. **去硬編碼 (De-hardcode)**：讓 DAG 面板 (Dashboard / Checklist) 動態讀取 `SKILL.md` 中定義的 Skill 名稱、描述與 Emoji。\\n2. **終端機體驗升級 (CLI UX)**：在執行各階段 (Phase) 時，隱藏如「切分文本」等不重要的細節（將其降級寫入背景 Log 檔），並透過 `rich.progress` 顯示當前處理進度、第 N 個檔案與 ETA 剩餘時間。\\n\\n---\\n\\n## Proposed Changes\\n\\n### 1. `core/orchestration/skill_registry.py` & `SkillManifest`\\n- 在 `SkillManifest` 新增 `display_name` 屬性。\\n- 在 `_load_metadata_from_md` 方法中，從 `SKILL.md` 的 YAML 解析 `metadata.openclaw.display_name` 與 `emoji`。若無設定，則退回預設使用 `emoji + skill_name` 的排版。\\n\\n### 2. `core/state/state_manager.py` (DAG Dashboard)\\n- 修改 `get_dashboard_text` 與 `_render_checklist`。\\n- 移除目前硬編碼的 `skill_display` 字典。\\n- 改為直接調用 `SkillRegistry` 取得目前 Skill 的 `manifest.display_name`。\\n- 使 `checklist.md` 與 Terminal Dashboard 的標題能自動對齊 `SKILL.md`。\\n\\n### 3. `core/orchestration/pipeline_base.py` (執行層 UI)\\n#### 引入 `rich.progress` 至 `process_tasks`\\n- 改寫 `process_tasks` 的迴圈，使用 `rich.progress.Progress` 包裝。\\n- 加入 `SpinnerColumn`, `BarColumn`, `MofNCompleteColumn`, `TimeElapsedColumn`, `TimeRemainingColumn` (ETA)。\\n- 範例格式：`🚀 Phase 1: Transcribe ━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━ 1/5 檔案 • 剩餘 2m 30s`\\n\\n#### Logging 分流\\n- 修改 `PipelineBase.log()` 方法。\\n- 若 `level == \\\"info\\\"`，預設應**不**直接 print 出來干擾 Progress Bar，而是透過 `progress.console.print` 或乾脆只寫入 log。\\n- 將原先許多\n<truncated 1774 bytes>","Description":"\"Create implementation plan for UI {"step_index":1313,"source":"MODEL","type":"VIEW_FILE","status":"DONE","created_at":"2026-05-29T10:13:57Z","content":"Created At: 2026-05-29T10:13:57Z\nCompleted At: 2026-05-29T10:13:57Z\nFile Path: `file:///Users/limchinkun/Desktop/local-workspace/openclaw-sandbox/core/orchestration/pipeline_base.py`\nTotal Lines: 771\nTotal Bytes: 32004\nShowing lines 1 to 771\nThe following code has been modified to include a line number before every line, in the format: <line_number>: <original_line>. Please note that any changes targeting the original code should remove the line number, colon, and leading space.\n1: \"\"\"\n2: PipelineBase — OpenClaw Shared Base Class (V2.3)\n3: =================================================\n4: 共用所有 Skill 的 OOP 基底。\n5: \n6: 關鍵升級（V2.3）：\n7: - 整合 SessionState 追蹤：所有控制狀態 (RUNNING/PAUSED/STOPPED/\n8:   FORCE_STOPPED/COMPLETED/FAILED) 持久化寫入 state/session.json\n9: - error() 方法現在在 log 檔中附加完整 Python traceback (exc_info=True)\n10: - build_logger 以 DEBUG 層級寫入檔案，INFO 層級印至 console\n11: \n12: 關鍵升級（V2.2）：\n13: - 新增 `skill_name` keyword 參數（預設 \"audio_transcriber\"，保持向後相容）\n14: - `base_dir` 動態對應 `data/{skill_name}/`\n15: - 所有現有 audio_transcriber phase 腳本無需修改即可繼續運行\n16: \n17: 用法（audio_transcriber，不需改動）:\n18:     super().__init__(\"p1\", \"Transcription\")   # skill_name 預設 audio_transcriber\n19: \n20: 用法（doc_parser）:\n21:     super().__init__(\"phase1a\", \"PDF Diagnostic\", skill_name=\"doc_parser\")\n22: \"\"\"\n23: \n24: import contextlib\n25: from dataclasses import dataclass, field\n26: import logging\n27: import os\n28: import signal\n29: import sys\n30: from typing import Any, Dict, List, Optional\n31: import uuid\n32: \n33: import psutil\n34: \n35: \n36: @dataclass\n37: class PipelineResponse:\n38:     \"\"\"Standardized response object for all Pipeline executions (P4.3-3).\"\"\"\n39: \n40:     status: str  # \"success\", \"failed\", \"paused\"\n41:     data: Dict[str, Any] = field(default_factory=dict)\n42:     errors: List[str] = field(default_factory=list)\n43: \n44: \n\n<truncated 32222 bytes>\nter phases start fresh\n731: \n732:             try:\n733:                 phase_obj.run(  # type: ignore[attr-defined]\n734:                     force=getattr(args, \"force\", False),\n735:                     subject=getattr(args, \"subject\", None),\n736:                     file_filter=getattr(args, \"file\", None),\n737:                     single_mode=getattr(args, \"single\", False),\n738:                     resume_from=phase_resume,\n739:                 )\n740:             except Exception as exc:\n741:                 phase_obj._write_session_state(SessionState.FAILED, context={\"error\": str(exc)})\n742:                 phase_obj.log(f\"💥 Phase {idx} 失敗: {exc}\", \"error\")\n743:                 any_stopped = True\n744:                 break\n745: \n746:             if phase_obj.stop_requested:\n747:                 if phase_obj.pause_requested:\n748:                     phase_obj._write_session_state(SessionState.PAUSED)\n749:                     phase_obj.log(\"💾 Pipeline 已暫停並儲存進度。\")\n750:                 else:\n751:                     phase_obj._write_session_state(SessionState.STOPPED)\n752:                     phase_obj.log(\"🛑 Pipeline 已停止。\")\n753:                 any_stopped = True\n754:                 break\n755: \n756:             phase_obj._write_session_state(SessionState.COMPLETED)\n757: \n758:             # DAG dashboard after each phase\n759:             if print_dashboard_between:\n760:                 phase_obj.state_manager.print_dashboard()\n761: \n762:             # Interactive pause between phases\n763:             if getattr(args, \"interactive\", False) and idx < len(phases):\n764:                 if sys.stdin.isatty():\n765:                     phase_obj.log(f\"✋ Phase {idx} 已完成。請按 [Enter] 繼續...\")\n766:                     input()\n767: \n768:         if not any_stopped:\n769:             print(\"🏁 Pipeline 執行完畢。\")\n770:             PipelineBase.notify_os(\"Pipeline 執行完畢\")\n771: \nThe above content shows the entire, complete file contents of the requested file.\n"}
{"step_index":1314,"source":"MODEL","type":"PLANNER_RESPONSE","status":"DONE","created_at":"2026-05-29T10:13:57Z","content":"好，現在我對 `process_tasks` 做全面的 `rich.progress` 升級：","tool_calls":[{"name":"multi_replace_file_content","args":{"Description":"\"Upgrade process_tasks to use rich.progress with ETA, item count, and clean terminal display. Also add a debug() passthrough in log() to keep terminal clean.\"","Instruction":"\"Replace the plain for-loop in process_tasks with a rich.progress bar showing item count and ETA. Also refactor log() so that messages prefixed with debug-level content only go to the log file.\"","ReplacementChunks":"[{\"AllowMultiple\":false,\"EndLine\":170,\"ReplacementContent\":\"    def log(self, msg: str, level: str = \\\"info\\\", console: bool = True) -> None:\\n        \\\"\\\"\\\"Log a message to the file and optionally to the terminal console.\\n\\n        Args:\\n            msg:     Message text.\\n            level:   Severity — 'debug', 'info', 'warn', 'error'.\\n            console: If False, write to log file only (no terminal output).\\n                     'debug' level always suppresses console output.\\n        \\\"\\\"\\\"\\n        # Terminal output — skip for debug or if console=False\\n        if level != \\\"debug\\\" and console:\\n            _progress_console = getattr(self, \\\"_rich_progress_console\\\", None)\\n            if _progress_console is not None:\\n                # Write through rich's console so the progress bar isn't broken\\n                _progress_console.print(msg)\\n            elif \\\"tqdm\\\" in sys.modules:\\n                import tqdm\\n                tqdm.tqdm.write(msg)\\n            else:\\n                print(msg)\\n\\n        # File logging — always write (debug goes here only)\\n        if self.logger:\\n            if level == \\\"info\\\":\\n                self.logger.info(msg)\\n            elif level == \\\"warn\\\":\\n                self.logger.warning(msg)\\n            elif level == \\\"error\\\":\\n                # exc_info=True captures the active exception traceback into the log file\\n                self.logger.error(msg, exc_info=True)\\n            elif level == \\\"debug\\\":\\n                self.logger.debug(msg)\\n\\n    def debug(self, msg: str) -> None:\\n        \\\"\\\"\\\"Write a debug-level message to the log file only (never to terminal).\\\"\\\"\\\"\\n        self.log(msg, level=\\\"debug\\\")\\n\\n    def info(self, msg: str) -> None:\\n        self.log(msg, level=\\\"info\\\")\\n\\n    def warning(self, msg: str) -> None:\\n        self.log(msg, level=\\\"warn\\\")\\n\\n    def error(self, msg: str) -> None:\\n        self.log(msg, level=\\\"error\\\")\",\"StartLine\":139,\"TargetContent\":\"    def log(self, msg: str, level: str = \\\"info\\\") -> None:\\\n<truncated 8319 bytes>","TargetFile":"\"/Users/limchinkun/Desktop/local-workspace/openclaw-sandbox/core/orchestration/pipeline_base.py\"","toolAction":"\"Upgrading process_tasks with rich progress\"","toolSummary":"\"Upgrade pipeline_base process_tasks\""}}]}

{"step_index":1335,"source":"MODEL","type":"PLANNER_RESPONSE","status":"DONE","created_at":"2026-05-29T10:17:15Z","tool_calls":[{"name":"write_to_file","args":{"ArtifactMetadata":"{\"ArtifactType\":\"task\",\"RequestFeedback\":false,\"Summary\":\"Task list for UI standardisation — all completed\"}","CodeContent":"\"# UI Standardisation 任務清單\\n\\n- `[x]` **1. 修改 `SkillManifest` 與解析**\\n  - `[x]` 在 `core/orchestration/skill_registry.py` 的 `SkillManifest` 新增 `display_name` 屬性。\\n  - `[x]` 在 `_load_metadata_from_md` 從 YAML 解析 `metadata.openclaw.display_name`，未設定時以 `emoji + skill_name` 作預設值。\\n\\n- `[x]` **2. 在所有的 `SKILL.md` 加入 `display_name`**\\n  - `[x]` 更新 15 個核心 Skill 的 `SKILL.md`（含 display_name 中文顯示名稱）。\\n\\n- `[x]` **3. 重構 `core/state/state_manager.py` (去硬編碼)**\\n  - `[x]` `get_dashboard_text` 動態讀取 `SkillRegistry` 取得 display_name。\\n  - `[x]` `_render_checklist` 動態讀取並統一 checklist.md 標題格式。\\n\\n- `[x]` **4. 重構 `core/orchestration/pipeline_base.py`**\\n  - `[x]` `log()` 新增 `console=False` 選項，並在 rich progress 執行期間自動透過 `progress.console` 輸出。\\n  - `[x]` `debug()` 明確只寫 log 檔，不輸出到終端機。\\n  - `[x]` 改寫 `process_tasks` 使用 `rich.progress.Progress`（含 Spinner、BarColumn、MofNCompleteColumn、TimeElapsed、TimeRemaining/ETA）。\\n  - `[x]` 處理完成後顯示「✅ [Phase名稱] 全部 N 個檔案處理完成」統一訊息。\\n\\n- `[x]` **5. 驗證**\\n  - `[x]` `pytest tests/` — 24 passed, 5 skipped ✅\\n  - `[x]` Registry smoke test — 14 skills 正確顯示 emoji + 中文名稱 ✅\"","Description":"\"Mark all tasks as completed\"","IsArtifact":"true","Overwrite":"true","TargetFile":"\"/Users/limchinkun/.gemini/antigravity/brain/32729395-ac4d-4d84-8be5-537c18482c2d/task.md\"","toolAction":"\"Marking all tasks complete\"","toolSummary":"\"Update task list\""}}]}

{"step_index":1337,"source":"MODEL","type":"PLANNER_RESPONSE","status":"DONE","created_at":"2026-05-29T10:17:37Z","tool_calls":[{"name":"write_to_file","args":{"ArtifactMetadata":"{\"ArtifactType\":\"walkthrough\",\"RequestFeedback\":false,\"Summary\":\"Walkthrough for UI standardisation: DAG dashboard de-hardcoded, rich progress bar with ETA added to pipeline execution\"}","CodeContent":"\"# Walkthrough — UI 統一化 (DAG Dashboard + 終端機進度)\\n\\n## 完成摘要\\n\\n### 1. 去硬編碼 — 動態 DAG 面板\\n\\n**問題**：`state_manager.py` 的 `get_dashboard_text()` 與 `_render_checklist()` 裡有手刻的 `dict`，Skill 名稱與面板標題不一致，新增 Skill 時必須手動修改核心程式。\\n\\n**解法**：\\n- 在 [`core/orchestration/skill_registry.py`](file:///Users/limchinkun/Desktop/local-workspace/openclaw-sandbox/core/orchestration/skill_registry.py) 的 `SkillManifest` 新增 `display_name: str = \\\"\\\"` 屬性。\\n- `_load_metadata_from_md()` 現在從 `SKILL.md` 的 YAML 讀取 `metadata.openclaw.display_name`，並自動前綴 `emoji`。\\n- [`core/state/state_manager.py`](file:///Users/limchinkun/Desktop/local-workspace/openclaw-sandbox/core/state/state_manager.py) 的兩個方法改為在執行時呼叫 `SkillRegistry` 取得 `manifest.display_name`，所有硬編碼字典已移除。\\n\\n**所有 `SKILL.md` 已新增 `display_name`**（15 個 Skill）。範例：\\n'''yaml\\nmetadata:\\n  openclaw:\\n    emoji: 🎙️\\n    display_name: 語音轉錄\\n'''\\n\\n---\\n\\n### 2. 終端機執行 UI 升級\\n\\n**問題**：終端機混雜了大量 debug 細節，使用者無法快速判斷目前執行進度與預估剩餘時間。\\n\\n**解法** — [`core/orchestration/pipeline_base.py`](file:///Users/limchinkun/Desktop/local-workspace/openclaw-sandbox/core/orchestration/pipeline_base.py)：\\n\\n#### `log()` 分流改良\\n- 新增 `console: bool = True` 參數；傳入 `console=False` 可讓訊息只寫入 log 檔。\\n- `debug()` 方法明確只寫入 log 檔，永不輸出到終端機。\\n- 在 `process_tasks` 執行期間，自動設定 `self._rich_progress_console`，讓 `self.log()` 透過 rich console 輸出而不破壞進度條。\\n\\n#### `process_tasks` 全面升級\\n終端機執行時現在顯示如下格式：\\n\\n'''\\n⠋ [P1 (轉錄)] [Math] lecture-01.m4a ━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━ 2/5 0:00:42 剩餘 1:02\\n'''\\n\\n元件說明：\\\n<truncated 1354 bytes>","Description":"\"Write final walkthrough\"","IsArtifact":"true","Overwrite":"true","TargetFile":"\"/Users/limchinkun/.gemini/antigravity/brain/32729395-ac4d-4d84-8be5-537c18482c2d/walkthrough.md\"","toolAction":"\"Writing final walkthrough\"","toolSummary":"\"Write walkthrough\""}}]}

{"step_index":1339,"source":"MODEL","type":"PLANNER_RESPONSE","status":"DONE","created_at":"2026-05-29T10:18:22Z","content":"全部完成！**24 passed, 5 skipped** ✅\n\n---\n\n## 本次變更摘要\n\n### 去硬編碼 (DAG 面板)\n- **`SkillManifest`** 新增 `display_name` 欄位，由 `SkillRegistry` 從每個 Skill 的 `SKILL.md` 自動解析 `metadata.openclaw.display_name` + `emoji`。\n- **`state_manager.py`** 的 `get_dashboard_text()` 與 `_render_checklist()` 中的手刻字典全部移除，改為執行時動態查詢 Registry。新增 Skill 後面板標題自動對齊，無需改任何核心程式。\n\n### 終端機執行 UI 升級\n- **`pipeline_base.py` `process_tasks`** 改用 `rich.progress`，每次執行時終端機顯示：\n  '''\n  ⠋ [P1 (轉錄)] [Math] lecture-01.m4a ━━━━━━━━━━━ 2/5  0:00:42 剩餘 1:02\n  '''\n- **`log()` 分流**：新增 `console=False` 選項；`debug()` 永遠只寫 log 檔，不干擾進度條。Progress 執行期間透過 `rich.console` 輸出，進度條不會被打斷。"}

{"step_index":1385,"source":"MODEL","type":"VIEW_FILE","status":"DONE","created_at":"2026-05-29T10:27:10Z","content":"Created At: 2026-05-29T10:27:10Z\nCompleted At: 2026-05-29T10:27:10Z\nFile Path: `file:///Users/limchinkun/Desktop/local-workspace/infra/scripts/start_bot.sh`\nTotal Lines: 37\nTotal Bytes: 1274\nShowing lines 1 to 37\nThe following code has been modified to include a line number before every line, in the format: <line_number>: <original_line>. Please note that any changes targeting the original code should remove the line number, colon, and leading space.\n1: #!/bin/bash\n2: # start_bot.sh - 獨立啟動 Telegram Bot (全能遙控中樞)\n3: \n4: GREEN='\\033[0;32m'\n5: BLUE='\\033[0;34m'\n6: YELLOW='\\033[1;33m'\n7: NC='\\033[0m'\n8: \n9: script_dir=\"$( cd \"$( dirname \"$0\" )\" && pwd )\"\n10: if [ \"$script_dir\" = \"/\" ] || [ -z \"$script_dir\" ] || [ \"$script_dir\" = \".\" ]; then\n11:     script_dir=\"$PWD\"\n12: fi\n13: INFRA_DIR=\"$(dirname \"$script_dir\")\"\n14: _LOCAL_WORKSPACE=\"$(dirname \"$INFRA_DIR\")\"\n15: WORKSPACE_DIR=\"${_LOCAL_WORKSPACE}/openclaw-sandbox\"\n16: \n17: export WORKSPACE_DIR\n18: export PATH=\"/opt/homebrew/bin:/usr/local/bin:${PATH}\"\n19: export PYTHONPATH=\"${_LOCAL_WORKSPACE}:${WORKSPACE_DIR}:${PYTHONPATH}\"\n20: \n21: LOG_DIR=\"${WORKSPACE_DIR}/logs\"\n22: mkdir -p \"$LOG_DIR\"\n23: BOT_DAEMON_PID_FILE=\"${LOG_DIR}/bot_daemon.pid\"\n24: \n25: echo -e \"${YELLOW}Starting Telegram Bot Daemon (Standalone)...${NC}\"\n26: if [[ -f \"${BOT_DAEMON_PID_FILE}\" ]] && kill -0 \"$(cat \"${BOT_DAEMON_PID_FILE}\")\" 2>/dev/null; then\n27:     echo -e \"   ${BLUE}─── ℹ️ Bot Daemon already running (PID: $(cat \"${BOT_DAEMON_PID_FILE}\"))${NC}\"\n28: else\n29:     (\n30:         cd \"${WORKSPACE_DIR}\" || exit\n31:         python3 skills/telegram_kb_agent/scripts/bot_daemon.py > \"${LOG_DIR}/bot_daemon.log\" 2>&1 &\n32:         echo $! > \"${BOT_DAEMON_PID_FILE}\"\n33:     )\n34:     sleep 1\n35:     echo -e \"   ${GREEN}✓ Bot Daemon started (PID: $(cat \"${BOT_DAEMON_PID_FILE}\"))${NC}\"\n36: fi\n37: \nThe above content shows the entire, complete file contents of the requested file.\n"}

{"step_index":1397,"source":"MODEL","type":"VIEW_FILE","status":"DONE","created_at":"2026-05-29T10:36:02Z","content":"Created At: 2026-05-29T10:36:02Z\nCompleted At: 2026-05-29T10:36:02Z\nFile Path: `file:///Users/limchinkun/Desktop/local-workspace/openclaw-sandbox/core/services/inbox_daemon.py`\nTotal Lines: 391\nTotal Bytes: 15531\nShowing lines 1 to 391\nThe following code has been modified to include a line number before every line, in the format: <line_number>: <original_line>. Please note that any changes targeting the original code should remove the line number, colon, and leading space.\n1: \"\"\"\n2: inbox_daemon.py — Open Claw System-Wide Inbox Monitor\n3: ======================================================\n4: Monitors the `inbox` canonical directories for all active skills using Watchdog.\n5: When a new file arrives (e.g. .m4a for audio_transcriber, .pdf for doc_parser),\n6: it triggers the target skill's pipeline via the WebUI API (with direct subprocess\n7: fallback when the WebUI is not running).\n8: \n9: Design:\n10:   - Watches the global `data/raw` directory.\n11:   - Routes files to specific skills based on extension (.m4a -> voice-memo, .pdf -> pdf-knowledge).\n12:   - File stability via size-polling (2s interval) with 300s timeout guard.\n13:   - Same-file debounce via threading.Event (cancellable).\n14: \"\"\"\n15: \n16: import json\n17: import logging\n18: import os\n19: import re\n20: import sys\n21: import threading\n22: import time\n23: from typing import Any, Optional\n24: \n25: # Path Resolution MUST happen before importing internal packages\n26: _core_dir = os.path.dirname(os.path.abspath(__file__))\n27: from core.utils.workspace import get_workspace_root\n28: \n29: _workspace_root = get_workspace_root()\n30: \n31: from core.cli.cli_runner import SkillRunner\n32: from core.orchestration.router_agent import RouterAgent, TaskManifest\n33: from core.orchestration.session_manifest import update_session_manifest\n34: from core.orchestration.skill_registry import SkillRegistry\n35: from core.utils.atomic_writer import AtomicWriter\n36: from core.utils.file_stability import FileStabilityPoller\n37: \n38: _logger = logging.getLogger(\"OpenClaw.InboxDaem\n<truncated 13843 bytes>\n: remaining_chain,\n338:                         \"subject\": subject,\n339:                         \"model\": env.get(\"OPENCLAW_ROUTER_MODEL\") if env else None,\n340:                     },\n341:                 )\n342:             )\n343: \n344:             # Remove the pending file so we don't trigger it again\n345:             os.remove(pending_path)\n346: \n347:             # File stability poller already debounces this, no need for manual lock\n348: \n349:         except Exception as e:\n350:             _logger.error(\n351:                 \"_check_proofreader_resume 發生未預期錯誤: %s — %s\", filepath, e, exc_info=True\n352:             )\n353: \n354:     def stop(self) -> None:\n355:         \"\"\"Gracefully stop the observer and all pending poll threads.\"\"\"\n356:         if self._observer:\n357:             self._observer.stop()\n358:             self._observer.join()\n359:             _logger.info(\"監控已停止\")\n360:         with self._lock:\n361:             self._seen_files.clear()\n362: \n363: \n364: if __name__ == \"__main__\":\n365:     import argparse\n366: \n367:     logging.basicConfig(\n368:         level=logging.INFO,\n369:         format=\"%(asctime)s [%(name)s] %(levelname)s %(message)s\",\n370:         datefmt=\"%Y-%m-%d %H:%M:%S\",\n371:     )\n372: \n373:     parser = argparse.ArgumentParser(description=\"Open Claw Inbox Daemon\")\n374:     parser.add_argument(\n375:         \"--scan-only\", action=\"store_true\", help=\"僅手動掃描並歸檔現有檔案，不啟動常駐監控\"\n376:     )\n377:     args = parser.parse_args()\n378: \n379:     daemon = SystemInboxDaemon()\n380:     if args.scan_only:\n381:         _logger.info(\"執行手動歸檔模式 (Scan Only)...\")\n382:         daemon.scan_all()\n383:     else:\n384:         daemon.start()\n385:         daemon.scan_all()  # Initial scan on startup\n386:         try:\n387:             while True:\n388:                 time.sleep(1)\n389:         except KeyboardInterrupt:\n390:             daemon.stop()\n391: \nThe above content shows the entire, complete file contents of the requested file.\n"}

{"step_index":1400,"source":"MODEL","type":"RUN_COMMAND","status":"DONE","created_at":"2026-05-29T10:36:11Z","content":"Created At: 2026-05-29T10:36:11Z\nCompleted At: 2026-05-29T10:36:11Z\n\n\t\t\t\tThe command completed successfully.\n\t\t\t\tOutput:\n\t\t\t\t    def dispatch(self, manifest: TaskManifest, dry_run: bool = False) -> Dict:\n        \"\"\"Dispatch a TaskManifest through the resolved skill chain.\"\"\"\n        chain = self.resolve(manifest)\n        results = []\n\n        logger.info(f\"🗺️  [RouterAgent] 路由計畫: {' → '.join(chain) or '(無匹配路由)'}\")\n\n        if dry_run or not chain:\n            return {\"plan\": chain, \"results\": []}\n\n        # Event-driven architecture: we ONLY enqueue the first skill in the chain.\n        # The subsequent skills are triggered via EventBus by the skill's manifest listener.\n        # But wait, RouterAgent only routes the file to the first skill's input directory,\n        # then enqueues the first skill.\n        first_skill = chain[0]\n        if self._registry is None:\n            results.append({\"skill\": first_skill, \"status\": \"skipped\", \"reason\": \"no registry\"})\n            return {\"plan\": chain, \"results\": results}\n\n        try:\n            skill_manifest = self._registry.get(first_skill)\n\n"}

{"step_index":1421,"source":"MODEL","type":"VIEW_FILE","status":"DONE","created_at":"2026-05-29T10:37:31Z","content":"Created At: 2026-05-29T10:37:31Z\nCompleted At: 2026-05-29T10:37:31Z\nFile Path: `file:///Users/limchinkun/Desktop/local-workspace/openclaw-sandbox/skills/audio_transcriber/SKILL.md`\nTotal Lines: 80\nTotal Bytes: 3003\nShowing lines 1 to 80\nThe following code has been modified to include a line number before every line, in the format: <line_number>: <original_line>. Please note that any changes targeting the original code should remove the line number, colon, and leading space.\n1: ---\n2: name: audio_transcriber\n3: description: End-to-end voice processing pipeline. Converts .m4a audio into polished,\n4:   MLX-Whisper transcribed Obsidian-ready study notes.\n5: metadata:\n6:   openclaw:\n7:     emoji: 🎙️\n8:     display_name: 語音轉錄\n9: state_tracking:\n10:   phases:\n11:   - p1\n12:   - p2\n13:   - p3\n14:   labels:\n15:     p1: P1 (轉錄)\n16:     p2: P2 (校對)\n17:     p3: P3 (合併)\n18: io_contracts:\n19:   consumes:\n20:   - audio/mp4\n21:   - audio/mpeg\n22:   - audio/wav\n23:   produces:\n24:   - text/markdown\n25: ---\n26: \n27: # Audio Transcriber Skill\n28: \n29: > **Pipeline**: M4A → Glossary → Transcription → Proofread → Merge\n30: \n31: ## Quick Start\n32: \n33: '''bash\n34: # 1. Drop audio files into the Universal Inbox\n35: cp lecture.m4a data/raw/<SubjectName>/\n36: \n37: # 2. Run in headless batch mode\n38: python3 skills/audio_transcriber/scripts/run_all.py --process-all\n39: \n40: # 3. Check pipeline progress\n41: cat data/audio_transcriber/state/checklist.md\n42: '''\n43: \n44: ## Anti-Hallucination Defense Architecture (V8.1)\n45: \n46: | Phase | Script | Function |\n47: |:---:|:---|:---|\n48: | P0 | `p00_glossary.py` | Glossary initialisation — prevents LLM from fabricating domain-specific terms |\n49: | P1 | `p01_transcribe.py` | High-fidelity transcription via MLX-Whisper. **VAD silence-trimming guard**: uses `pydub.silence` to pre-strip noise (Requires `ffmpeg` installed on system to decode audio files). If `silence_ratio > max_removal_ratio` (default: 0.90) or ffmpeg decoding fails, automatically falls back to the raw audio to prevent over-trimming. Includes multi-clip majority-vote language detection. |\n50: | P2 | `p02_glossary_apply.py` | Automatically applies glossary constraints to the raw transcript to ensure terminology accuracy before proofreading. |\n51: | P3 | `p03_merge.py` | Cross-segment consolidation and refinement. |\n52: | Handoff | (Autonomous) | The RouterAgent automatically forwards the merged transcript to the `proofreader` skill for AI verification against reference material and dashboard review. |\n53: \n54: ## Common Commands\n55: \n56: '''bash\n57: # Run full pipeline on all pending files\n58: python3 skills/audio_transcriber/scripts/run_all.py --process-all\n59: \n60: # Run from a specific phase\n61: python3 skills/audio_transcriber/scripts/run_all.py --from 2\n62: \n63: # Force re-run on all files\n64: python3 skills/audio_transcriber/scripts/run_all.py --process-all --force\n65: \n66: # Regenerate glossary only\n67: python3 skills/audio_transcriber/scripts/run_all.py --glossary\n68: \n69: # Process a single subject\n70: python3 skills/audio_transcriber/scripts/run_all.py --subject <SubjectName>\n71: '''\n72: \n73: ## Global Standards\n74: \n75: - **Zero Temperature**: `config.yaml` enforces `temperature: 0` to guarantee deterministic, hallucination-free outputs.\n76: - **Headless CLI**: Supports `--process-all`, `--from`, `--force`, `--resume`, `--log-json` for full CI/CD compatibility.\n77: - **Preflight Check**: Validates all dependencies and config on every run before processing begins.\n78: - **Checkpoint Resume**: All phase completions are saved to `state/`; use `--resume` to continue after an interruption.\n79: - macOS native notifications (`osascript`) and graceful `KeyboardInterrupt` handling with checkpoint save.\n80: \nThe above content shows the entire, complete file contents of the requested file.\n"}

{"step_index":1436,"source":"MODEL","type":"RUN_COMMAND","status":"DONE","created_at":"2026-05-29T10:43:07Z","content":"Created At: 2026-05-29T10:43:07Z\nCompleted At: 2026-05-29T10:43:07Z\n\n\t\t\t\tThe command completed successfully.\n\t\t\t\tOutput:\n\t\t\t\t    def _load_metadata_from_md(self, skill_dir: str, manifest: SkillManifest) -> None:\n        \"\"\"Parse SKILL.md YAML frontmatter to extract state_tracking and io_contracts.\"\"\"\n        import yaml\n\n        skill_md = os.path.join(skill_dir, \"SKILL.md\")\n        if not os.path.exists(skill_md):\n            return\n\n        try:\n            with open(skill_md, encoding=\"utf-8\") as f:\n                content = f.read()\n            if content.startswith(\"---\"):\n                end_idx = content.find(\"---\", 3)\n                if end_idx != -1:\n                    yaml_content = content[3:end_idx]\n                    data = yaml.safe_load(yaml_content) or {}\n                    \n                    # Extract UI Display Name & Emoji\n                    metadata = data.get(\"metadata\", {}).get(\"openclaw\", {})\n                    emoji = metadata.get(\"emoji\", \"\")\n                    display_name = metadata.get(\"display_name\", \"\")\n\n"}

{"step_index":1449,"source":"MODEL","type":"PLANNER_RESPONSE","status":"DONE","created_at":"2026-05-29T10:43:22Z","thinking":"**Prioritizing Tool Usage**\n\nI'm now focusing on tool selection, emphasizing specific tools over general ones where possible. It should've found the file, so now to trace execution of the file searching.\n\n\n**Tracing State Updates**\n\nI'm now tracing the logic behind `\"note\": \"更新/新增\"` in `.pipeline_state.json`. Specifically, I'm trying to understand why a file name `fname` might not be in the state dictionary. I've ruled out the beginning of `sync_physical_files()`, which should retain the state. I suspect there may be a logical flow problem in how the physical files are handled, which may lead to incorrect detection of files. I'm now digging into possible removals of files that should exist.\n\n\n","tool_calls":[{"name":"run_command","args":{"CommandLine":"\"cat core/state/state_manager.py | grep -A 40 \\\"def sync_physical_files\\\"\"","Cwd":"\"/Users/limchinkun/Desktop/local-workspace/openclaw-sandbox\"","WaitMsBeforeAsync":"5000","toolAction":"\"Check sync_physical_files\"","toolSummary":"\"Check sync logic\""}}]}
{"step_index":1450,"source":"MODEL","type":"RUN_COMMAND","status":"DONE","created_at":"2026-05-29T10:43:33Z","content":"Created At: 2026-05-29T10:43:33Z\nCompleted At: 2026-05-29T10:43:33Z\n\n\t\t\t\tThe command completed successfully.\n\t\t\t\tOutput:\n\t\t\t\t    def sync_physical_files(self):\n        \"\"\"Scans raw_data/ for .m4a files and updates internal state.\"\"\"\n        with self._lock:\n            if not os.path.exists(self.raw_dir):\n                return\n\n            if self.skill_name == \"interactive_reader\":\n                # Flat directory in data/wiki\n                subjects_dirs = [(\"Wiki\", self.raw_dir)]\n            else:\n                subjects_dirs = [\n                    (d, os.path.join(self.raw_dir, d))\n                    for d in os.listdir(self.raw_dir)\n                    if os.path.isdir(os.path.join(self.raw_dir, d))\n                ]\n\n            for subj, subj_path in subjects_dirs:\n                if subj not in self.state:\n                    self.state[subj] = {}\n\n                import glob\n\n                physical_files = []\n                if isinstance(self.file_ext, (tuple, list)):\n                    for ext in self.file_ext:\n                        glob_ext = f\"*{ext}\" if ext.startswith(\".\") else ext\n                        physical_files.extend(glob.glob(os.path.join(subj_path, glob_ext)))\n                else:\n                    glob_ext = (\n                        f\"*{self.file_ext}\" if self.file_ext.startswith(\".\") else self.file_ext\n                    )\n                    physical_files = glob.glob(os.path.join(subj_path, glob_ext))\n\n                for pf in physical_files:\n                    fname = os.path.basename(pf)\n                    if fname == \"correction_log.md\":\n                        continue\n                    fhash = self.get_file_hash(pf)\n                    mtime = datetime.fromtimestamp(os.path.getmtime(pf)).strftime(\"%Y-%m-%d\")\n\n                    if fname not in self.state[subj]:\n\n"}

{"step_index":1453,"source":"MODEL","type":"RUN_COMMAND","status":"DONE","created_at":"2026-05-29T10:43:38Z","content":"Created At: 2026-05-29T10:43:38Z\nCompleted At: 2026-05-29T10:43:55Z\n\n\t\t\t\tThe command completed successfully.\n\t\t\t\tOutput:\n\t\t\t\t            for subj, subj_path in subjects_dirs:\n                if subj not in self.state:\n                    self.state[subj] = {}\n\n                import glob\n\n                physical_files = []\n                if isinstance(self.file_ext, (tuple, list)):\n                    for ext in self.file_ext:\n                        glob_ext = f\"*{ext}\" if ext.startswith(\".\") else ext\n                        physical_files.extend(glob.glob(os.path.join(subj_path, glob_ext)))\n                else:\n                    glob_ext = (\n                        f\"*{self.file_ext}\" if self.file_ext.startswith(\".\") else self.file_ext\n                    )\n                    physical_files = glob.glob(os.path.join(subj_path, glob_ext))\n\n                for pf in physical_files:\n                    fname = os.path.basename(pf)\n                    if fname == \"correction_log.md\":\n                        continue\n                    fhash = self.get_file_hash(pf)\n                    mtime = datetime.fromtimestamp(os.path.getmtime(pf)).strftime(\"%Y-%m-%d\")\n\n                    if fname not in self.state[subj]:\n                        self.state[subj][fname] = {\n                            **dict.fromkeys(self.PHASES, \"⏳\"),\n                            \"hash\": fhash,\n                            \"date\": mtime,\n                            \"note\": \"更新/新增\",\n                            \"output_hashes\": {},\n                            \"char_count\": {},\n                        }\n                    else:\n                        # If raw audio changed, negate everything\n                        if self.state[subj][fname].get(\"hash\") != fhash:\n                            self.state[subj][fname].update(\n                                {\n                                    **dict.fromkeys(self.PHASES, \"⏳\"),\n                                    \"hash\": fhash,\n                                    \"date\": mtime,\n                                    \"note\": \"原始檔已變更\",\n                                }\n                            )\n            self._save_state()\n\n    def cascade_invalidate(self, subject: str, filename: str, changed_phase: str):\n        \"\"\"Invalidate dependent phases. E.g. if p1 output changes, p2-p5 become ⏳.\"\"\"\n        with self._lock:\n            if subject not in self.state or filename not in self.state[subject]:\n                return\n\n"}

{"step_index":1456,"source":"MODEL","type":"RUN_COMMAND","status":"DONE","created_at":"2026-05-29T10:44:00Z","content":"Created At: 2026-05-29T10:44:00Z\nCompleted At: 2026-05-29T10:44:26Z\n\n\t\t\t\tThe command completed successfully.\n\t\t\t\tOutput:\n\t\t\t\t        if os.path.exists(self.state_file):\n            try:\n                with open(self.state_file, encoding=\"utf-8\") as f:\n                    fcntl.flock(f, fcntl.LOCK_SH)  # Shared read lock\n                    try:\n                        raw = json.load(f)\n                    finally:\n                        fcntl.flock(f, fcntl.LOCK_UN)\n                if isinstance(raw, dict):\n                    self._checkpoint = raw.get(\"_checkpoint\")\n                    if isinstance(raw.get(\"_state\"), dict):\n                        return raw.get(\"_state\", {})\n                    return {k: v for k, v in raw.items() if not str(k).startswith(\"_\")}\n            except json.JSONDecodeError as exc:\n                _logger.error(\n                    \"狀態檔案 JSON 損毀，將以空狀態啟動 (%s): %s\",\n                    self.state_file,\n                    exc,\n                    exc_info=True,\n                )\n            except OSError as exc:\n                _logger.error(\"無法讀取狀態檔案 (%s): %s\", self.state_file, exc, exc_info=True)\n            except Exception as exc:\n                _logger.error(\n                    \"讀取狀態檔案時發生未預期錯誤 (%s): %s\", self.state_file, exc, exc_info=True\n                )\n        return {}\n\n    def _save_state(self):\n        \"\"\"Persist state to JSON with exclusive write lock, then re-render checklist.\"\"\"\n        with self._lock:\n\n"}

{"step_index":1462,"source":"MODEL","type":"RUN_COMMAND","status":"DONE","created_at":"2026-05-29T10:44:38Z","content":"Created At: 2026-05-29T10:44:38Z\nCompleted At: 2026-05-29T10:44:39Z\n\n\t\t\t\tThe command completed successfully.\n\t\t\t\tOutput:\n\t\t\t\t\n\ndef test_load_state_corrupt_json(tmp_workspace):\n    # Setup corrupted JSON\n    state_file = os.path.join(tmp_workspace, \"dashboard_state.json\")\n    with open(state_file, \"w\", encoding=\"utf-8\") as f:\n        f.write(\"{ invalid json\")\n\n--\n\n\ndef test_update_task_cascade_invalidation(tmp_workspace):\n    sm = StateManager(tmp_workspace, \"doc_parser\")\n\n    # Manually seed state\n    sm.state = {\"Math\": {\"file1.pdf\": {\"p1a\": \"✅\", \"p1b\": \"✅\", \"p1c\": \"✅\"}}}\n\n--\n\n@patch(\"core.state.state_manager.AtomicWriter.write_text\")\ndef test_render_checklist_atomic(mock_write_text, tmp_workspace):\n    sm = StateManager(tmp_workspace, \"doc_parser\")\n    sm.state = {\"Math\": {\"test.pdf\": {}}}\n    sm.update_task(\"Math\", \"test.pdf\", \"p1a\", \"✅\")\n\n    # _save_state automatically calls _render_checklist\n\n"}

{"step_index":1465,"source":"MODEL","type":"VIEW_FILE","status":"DONE","created_at":"2026-05-29T10:44:46Z","content":"Created At: 2026-05-29T10:44:46Z\nCompleted At: 2026-05-29T10:44:46Z\nFile Path: `file:///Users/limchinkun/Desktop/local-workspace/openclaw-sandbox/core/orchestration/router_agent.py`\nTotal Lines: 560\nTotal Bytes: 23608\nShowing lines 1 to 560\nThe following code has been modified to include a line number before every line, in the format: <line_number>: <original_line>. Please note that any changes targeting the original code should remove the line number, colon, and leading space.\n1: \"\"\"\n2: core/router_agent.py — Cross-Skill Dynamic Orchestration (#13)\n3: ==============================================================\n4: Strategic scaffold for the Router Agent, which chains multiple Skills\n5: into automated end-to-end workflows based on file type and user intent.\n6: \n7: CURRENT STATUS: Scaffold / Interface Definition\n8:   This file defines the data contracts and orchestration skeleton.\n9:   Actual skill invocation wiring is deferred to P3 implementation.\n10: \n11: Usage (future):\n12:     from core.orchestration.router_agent import RouterAgent, TaskManifest\n13: \n14:     manifest = TaskManifest(\n15:         source_path=\"/path/to/lecture.m4a\",\n16:         intent=\"transcribe_and_compile\",\n17:     )\n18:     RouterAgent().dispatch(manifest)\n19: \"\"\"\n20: \n21: from __future__ import annotations\n22: \n23: from dataclasses import dataclass, field\n24: import json\n25: import os\n26: import sys\n27: from typing import Any, Callable, Dict, List, Optional\n28: import logging\n29: \n30: logger = logging.getLogger(\"OpenClaw.RouterAgent\")\n31: \n32: from core.orchestration.event_bus import DomainEvent, EventBus\n33: from core.orchestration.session_manifest import update_session_manifest\n34: from core.orchestration.task_queue import task_queue\n35: from core.state.global_registry import GlobalRegistry\n36: \n37: # ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n38: # Data Contracts\n39: # ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n40: \n41: \n42: @dataclass\n43: class TaskManifest:\n44:     \"\n<truncated 22770 bytes>\nwd = os.path.join(\n516:                 os.path.dirname(os.path.dirname(os.path.dirname(os.path.abspath(__file__)))),\n517:                 \"skills\",\n518:                 first_skill,\n519:             )\n520: \n521:             # Build command from cli_entry or fallback\n522:             if skill_manifest.cli_entry:\n523:                 script_path = os.path.join(cwd, skill_manifest.cli_entry)\n524:             else:\n525:                 script_path = os.path.join(cwd, \"scripts\", \"run_all.py\")\n526: \n527:             cmd = [sys.executable, script_path]\n528:             # Some skills expect --process-all (e.g. doc_parser)\n529:             if first_skill == \"doc_parser\":\n530:                 cmd.append(\"--process-all\")\n531: \n532:             task_queue.enqueue(\n533:                 name=f\"{first_skill} Pipeline\",\n534:                 cmd=cmd,\n535:                 cwd=cwd,\n536:                 filepath=target_path,\n537:                 skill=first_skill,\n538:                 chain=chain,\n539:                 subject=manifest.subject or \"Default\",\n540:                 env={\"OPENCLAW_ROUTER_MODEL\": manifest.model} if manifest.model else None,\n541:             )\n542: \n543:             workspace_root = os.path.dirname(\n544:                 os.path.dirname(os.path.dirname(os.path.abspath(__file__)))\n545:             )\n546:             update_session_manifest(\n547:                 workspace_root,\n548:                 manifest.subject or \"Default\",\n549:                 os.path.basename(target_path),\n550:                 first_skill,\n551:                 \"processing\",\n552:             )\n553: \n554:             results.append({\"skill\": first_skill, \"status\": \"enqueued\"})\n555:         except Exception as exc:\n556:             results.append({\"skill\": first_skill, \"status\": \"error\", \"error\": str(exc)})\n557:             logger.info(f\"❌ [RouterAgent] {first_skill} 分發失敗: {exc}\")\n558: \n559:         return {\"plan\": chain, \"results\": results}\n560: \nThe above content shows the entire, complete file contents of the requested file.\n"}

{"step_index":1467,"source":"MODEL","type":"PLANNER_RESPONSE","status":"DONE","created_at":"2026-05-29T10:44:46Z","thinking":"**Prioritizing Tool Usage**\n\nI'm now focusing on tool selection, emphasizing specific tools over general ones where possible. The goal is to optimize efficiency by leveraging tools tailored to particular tasks, like viewing files. This strategy promises a more targeted and less error-prone approach to problem-solving, which I will continue to refine and implement moving forward.\n\n\n**Analyzing File System Interactions**\n\nI'm now investigating file system interactions to explain the repeated restarts of key processes. Specifically, I'm examining the behavior of `InboxDaemon.scan_all()` to understand why it seems to be reprocessing files that should have already been moved by `os.rename()`. The focus is on verifying if the scanning process truly skips moved files and determining the root cause of these unexpected restarts, which is crucial for efficient operation.\n\n\n**Investigating Dashboard Perceptions**\n\nI'm now focusing on how the dashboard's display of process states, particularly the \"⏳\" indicator, creates the *perception* of restarts. It seems the visual representation, influenced by the `.pipeline_state.json` file, is critical. Even if the pipeline isn't actively running, a reset of the `.pipeline_state.json` to \"⏳\" by a background process, like the `StateManager` during `uv run python verify_registry`, misleads the user to THINK that the entire process has restarted, even if it has not.\n\n\n","tool_calls":[{"name":"run_command","args":{"CommandLine":"\"cat core/cli/check_status.py | head -n 30\"","Cwd":"\"/Users/limchinkun/Desktop/local-workspace/openclaw-sandbox\"","WaitMsBeforeAsync":"5000","toolAction":"\"Check check_status.py\"","toolSummary":"\"Check status.py\""}}]}
{"step_index":1468,"source":"MODEL","type":"RUN_COMMAND","status":"DONE","created_at":"2026-05-29T10:44:55Z","content":"Created At: 2026-05-29T10:44:55Z\nCompleted At: 2026-05-29T10:44:55Z\n\n\t\t\t\tThe command completed successfully.\n\t\t\t\tOutput:\n\t\t\t\t\"\"\"\ncore/check_status.py — 全系統狀態報告產生器\n==========================================\n提供給 Telegram Bot 或是 CLI 使用，一次性輸出所有核心 Skill 的進度儀表板。\n\"\"\"\n\nimport os\n\n# from core.utils.log_manager import build_logger\n# logger = build_logger(__name__, console=True)\nfrom core.utils.workspace import get_workspace_root\n\n_workspace_root = get_workspace_root()\n\nfrom core.state.state_manager import StateManager\nfrom core.utils.path_builder import PathBuilder\n\n\ndef get_full_status_report() -> str:\n    \"\"\"產生包含多個 Skill 的聯合儀表板報告。\"\"\"\n    report_lines = []\n\n    # 動態掃描所有有狀態的 Skill\n    data_dir = os.path.join(_workspace_root, \"data\")\n    skills_to_check = []\n    if os.path.isdir(data_dir):\n        for d in sorted(os.listdir(data_dir)):\n            # Ignore global state\n            if d == \"_global_\":\n                continue\n\n"}

{"step_index":1483,"source":"MODEL","type":"VIEW_FILE","status":"DONE","created_at":"2026-05-29T10:45:27Z","content":"Created At: 2026-05-29T10:45:27Z\nCompleted At: 2026-05-29T10:45:27Z\nFile Path: `file:///Users/limchinkun/Desktop/local-workspace/openclaw-sandbox/core/orchestration/skill_registry.py`\nTotal Lines: 221\nTotal Bytes: 8961\nShowing lines 1 to 221\nThe following code has been modified to include a line number before every line, in the format: <line_number>: <original_line>. Please note that any changes targeting the original code should remove the line number, colon, and leading space.\n1: \"\"\"\n2: core/skill_registry.py — Dynamic Plugin Discovery Architecture (#17)\n3: ====================================================================\n4: Enables zero-friction extensibility: new Skills are auto-discovered\n5: by scanning the skills/ directory at startup for a manifest.py file\n6: that conforms to the SkillManifest protocol.\n7: \n8: CURRENT STATUS: Fully functional discovery + registration.\n9:   Async execution wiring (#16) and CLI/Telegram auto-population\n10:   are the remaining P3 integration tasks.\n11: \n12: Usage:\n13:     from core.orchestration.skill_registry import SkillRegistry\n14: \n15:     registry = SkillRegistry(skills_root=\"/path/to/skills\")\n16:     registry.discover()\n17: \n18:     for name, manifest in registry.all().items():\n19:         logger.info(f\"{name}: {manifest.description}\")\n20: \n21:     run_fn = registry.get_run_fn(\"audio_transcriber\")\n22:     run_fn(subject=\"math\", force=False)\n23: \"\"\"\n24: \n25: from __future__ import annotations\n26: \n27: from dataclasses import dataclass, field\n28: import importlib.util\n29: import os\n30: import sys\n31: from typing import Callable, Dict, List, Optional\n32: \n33: import logging\n34: \n35: logger = logging.getLogger(\"OpenClaw.skill_registry\")\n36: \n37: # ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n38: # SkillManifest Protocol (#17)\n39: # ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n40: \n41: \n42: @dataclass\n43: class SkillManifest:\n44:     \"\"\"Interface contract every discoverable Skill must implement.\n45: \n46:     \n<truncated 6429 bytes>\nore[union-attr]\n179:         finally:\n180:             sys.path.remove(skill_dir)\n181: \n182:         manifest = getattr(mod, \"MANIFEST\", None)\n183:         if not isinstance(manifest, SkillManifest):\n184:             raise TypeError(\n185:                 f\"manifest.py must expose MANIFEST: SkillManifest, got {type(manifest)}\"\n186:             )\n187:         return manifest\n188: \n189:     def register(self, manifest: SkillManifest) -> None:\n190:         \"\"\"Manually register a SkillManifest (for testing or programmatic use).\"\"\"\n191:         self._registry[manifest.skill_name] = manifest\n192: \n193:     def get(self, skill_name: str) -> Optional[SkillManifest]:\n194:         \"\"\"Return a SkillManifest by name, or None if not found.\"\"\"\n195:         return self._registry.get(skill_name)\n196: \n197:     def get_run_fn(self, skill_name: str) -> Callable:\n198:         \"\"\"Return the run callable for a skill, raising KeyError if missing.\"\"\"\n199:         manifest = self._registry.get(skill_name)\n200:         if manifest is None:\n201:             raise KeyError(\n202:                 f\"Skill '{skill_name}' not registered. Run SkillRegistry.discover() first.\"\n203:             )\n204:         if manifest.run_fn is None:\n205:             raise NotImplementedError(\n206:                 f\"Skill '{skill_name}' has no run_fn defined in its manifest.\"\n207:             )\n208:         return manifest.run_fn\n209: \n210:     def all(self) -> Dict[str, SkillManifest]:\n211:         \"\"\"Return a copy of the full registry.\"\"\"\n212:         return dict(self._registry)\n213: \n214:     def list_names(self) -> List[str]:\n215:         \"\"\"Return sorted list of all registered skill names.\"\"\"\n216:         return sorted(self._registry.keys())\n217: \n218:     def for_file_type(self, ext: str) -> List[SkillManifest]:\n219:         \"\"\"Return skills that can handle a given file extension.\"\"\"\n220:         return [m for m in self._registry.values() if ext.lower() in m.file_types]\n221: \nThe above content shows the entire, complete file contents of the requested file.\n"}

I need to understand how my changes in `_load_metadata_from_md` might have affected this process.\n\n\n**Investigating File Type Discovery**\n\nI've traced the issue to `StateManager.__init__` within the `SkillRegistry`. {"step_index":1495,"source":"MODEL","type":"RUN_COMMAND","status":"DONE","created_at":"2026-05-29T10:46:06Z","content":"Created At: 2026-05-29T10:46:06Z\nCompleted At: 2026-05-29T10:52:55Z\n\n\t\t\t\tThe command completed successfully.\n\t\t\t\tOutput:\n\t\t\t\t                    fcntl.flock(lock_fd, fcntl.LOCK_UN)\n        self._render_checklist()\n\n    def _serialize_state(self) -> Dict[str, Any]:\n        payload: Dict[str, Any] = {subject: files for subject, files in self.state.items()}\n        if self._checkpoint:\n            payload[\"_checkpoint\"] = self._checkpoint\n        return payload\n\n    def get_file_hash(self, filepath: str) -> str:\n        \"\"\"Return SHA-256 hash of a file.\"\"\"\n        if not os.path.exists(filepath):\n            return \"\"\n        sha256 = hashlib.sha256()\n        with open(filepath, \"rb\") as f:\n            for block in iter(lambda: f.read(4096), b\"\"):\n                sha256.update(block)\n        return sha256.hexdigest()\n\n    def sync_physical_files(self):\n        \"\"\"Scans raw_data/ for .m4a files and updates internal state.\"\"\"\n        with self._lock:\n            if not os.path.exists(self.raw_dir):\n                return\n\n            if self.skill_name == \"interactive_reader\":\n                # Flat directory in data/wiki\n                subjects_dirs = [(\"Wiki\", self.raw_dir)]\n            else:\n                subjects_dirs = [\n                    (d, os.path.join(self.raw_dir, d))\n                    for d in os.listdir(self.raw_dir)\n                    if os.path.isdir(os.path.join(self.raw_dir, d))\n                ]\n\n            for subj, subj_path in subjects_dirs:\n\n"}

{"step_index":1522,"source":"MODEL","type":"RUN_COMMAND","status":"DONE","created_at":"2026-05-29T10:56:57Z","content":"Created At: 2026-05-29T10:56:57Z\nCompleted At: 2026-05-29T10:56:57Z\n\n\t\t\t\tThe command completed successfully.\n\t\t\t\tOutput:\n\t\t\t\t\n        registry = SkillRegistry()\n        registry.discover()\n        manifest = registry.get(skill_name)\n        if manifest:\n            self.PHASES = manifest.phases or [\"p1\"]\n            self._phase_labels = manifest.phase_labels or {p: p.upper() for p in self.PHASES}\n\n            # Map file extensions\n            if len(manifest.file_types) == 1:\n                self.file_ext = manifest.file_types[0]\n            elif len(manifest.file_types) > 1:\n                self.file_ext = tuple(manifest.file_types)\n            else:\n                self.file_ext = \"*.md\"\n        canonical_state_dir = os.path.join(base_dir, \"state\")\n        legacy_state_file = os.path.join(base_dir, \".pipeline_state.json\")\n        legacy_checklist_file = os.path.join(base_dir, \"checklist.md\")\n        canonical_state_file = os.path.join(canonical_state_dir, \".pipeline_state.json\")\n        canonical_checklist_file = os.path.join(canonical_state_dir, \"checklist.md\")\n\n        self.state_file = (\n            canonical_state_file\n            if os.path.exists(canonical_state_file) or not os.path.exists(legacy_state_file)\n            else legacy_state_file\n\n"}

{"step_index":1528,"source":"MODEL","type":"RUN_COMMAND","status":"DONE","created_at":"2026-05-29T10:57:22Z","content":"Created At: 2026-05-29T10:57:22Z\nCompleted At: 2026-05-29T10:57:26Z\n\n\t\t\t\tThe command completed successfully.\n\t\t\t\tOutput:\n\t\t\t\t                    else:\n                        done_tasks.append(task)\n                else:\n                    all_tasks.append(task)\n\n        all_tasks.sort(key=lambda t: (t[\"subject\"], t[\"filename\"]))\n        done_tasks.sort(key=lambda t: (t[\"subject\"], t[\"filename\"]))\n\n        if done_tasks:\n            import sys\n\n            if \"--process-all\" in sys.argv:\n                reprocess_set = {}\n            else:\n                reprocess_set = self._batch_select_reprocess(done_tasks)\n            skipped = len(done_tasks) - len(reprocess_set)\n            if skipped > 0:\n                self.log(f\"⏭️  共 {skipped} 個已完成檔案被跳過。\")\n            all_tasks = sorted(\n                all_tasks + list(reprocess_set.values()),\n                key=lambda t: (t[\"subject\"], t[\"filename\"]),\n            )\n\n        if resume_from:\n            cp_subj = resume_from.get(\"subject\", \"\")\n            cp_fname = resume_from.get(\"filename\", \"\")\n            start_idx = 0\n            for i, t in enumerate(all_tasks):\n                if t[\"subject\"] == cp_subj and t[\"filename\"] == cp_fname:\n                    start_idx = i\n                    break\n\n"}

The goal is to optimize efficiency by leveraging tools tailored to particular tasks, like viewing files. This strategy promises a more targeted and less error-prone approach to problem-solving, which I will continue to refine and implement moving forward.\n\n\n**Refining Tool Choices**\n\nI'm now assessing specific tool usage, having identified several potential tools. I will never use `cat` inside a bash command for file creation or appending, nor will I run `grep` within bash commands unless necessary. I'm prioritizing direct tool execution for listing, viewing, finding, and replacing. Before any execution, I must list and consider all related tools, executing a set only if they are the most suitable. I'm focusing on why I did not have `.bak` files. I understand that the `doc_parser` ran successfully, but my bug caused issues.\n\n\n**Analyzing Root Cause**\n\nI've pinpointed the root cause of the `doc_parser` reset. It stems from a previous version where the `StateManager` didn't dynamically load `manifest` using `SkillRegistry`. Specifically, in my older design, `StateManager` took `file_ext` as an argument during initialization, not dynamically. Consequently, if `run_all.py` didn't explicitly pass a `file_ext` (it passed None), the code defaulted to \"*.md\". Before my changes, the `SkillRegistry` was correctly providing `manifest.file_types` for `audio_transcriber`.\n\n\n**Investigating File Extensions**\n\nI'm now diving into the file extension issue and trying to understand why the bug occurred despite seemingly correct file extension handling.
